<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Business overview </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies has advanced R&amp;D activities in countries including Finland, the United Kingdom, and the United
States. Nokia Technologies also collaborates in open innovation with universities and research institutes globally. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies holds several central roles
in standardization bodies and contributes to standardization work by filing technical proposals which, when found relevant, are often accepted and embodied in standards. In addition, Nokia Technologies develops reference implementations while
defining the standards, which result in significant innovations covering proprietary ways to implement relevant technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">For examples of Nokia
Technologies&#146; R&amp;D achievements in 2014 refer to &#147;Operating and financial review and prospects&#151;Main events in 2014&#151;Nokia Technologies operating highlights&#148;. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:16pt; font-family:ARIAL">&#147;Nokia Technologies sees opportunities in licensing its proprietary technologies, intellectual property and brand assets into telecommunications and adjacent
industries.&#148; </P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Patents and licenses </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We
manage an IP portfolio with approximately 10&nbsp;000 patent families comprised of approximately 30&nbsp;000 individual patents and patent applications. In industry terms, our portfolio is relatively young: ten years from now, two-thirds of our
current patents will continue to be in force. We continue to renew our patent portfolio with innovations produced by our strong R&amp;D team. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Competition
</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our current patent portfolio spans a number of technology categories including radio connectivity and networking, multimedia, user interface (&#147;UI&#148;)
and software, hardware, product, and mapping and location services. As Nokia Technologies expands its successful licensing program to cover patents which have not been broadly licensed to date, as well as proprietary technologies and other
intellectual property, it could face competition from alternate technologies or solutions. However, it is too early to anticipate which of these may be significant in future. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:16pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Breakdown of Nokia Technologies&#146; IP portfolio </B></P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">


<IMG SRC="g861743page121.jpg" ALT="LOGO">
 </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:20pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_13"></A>Discontinued operations </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business sold to Microsoft </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Segment overview </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">With the closing of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Nokia
exited the business of selling mobile devices. Prior to the Sale, Nokia created products for virtually every demographic, with sales in more than 160 countries. The Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business was comprised of two business units: Mobile
Phones, which focused on the area of mass market entry and feature phones as well as affordable smartphones; and Smart Devices, which focused on advanced products, including smartphones powered by the Windows Phone operating system. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Mobile Phones </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Mobile Phones unit developed ultra-low cost phones,
feature phones and affordable smartphones, with most models running the Nokia Asha software platform and the Series 30+ operating system. In early 2014, the unit also introduced its first affordable smartphones based on the Nokia X software
platform, which offers access to Android applications. The major Mobile Phones development sites were located in Beijing, China and Oulu, Finland. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The strategy of
the Mobile Phones unit focused on leveraging the company&#146;s innovation and strength in growth markets to provide people with an affordable internet experience on their mobile device. The most affordable products were targeted at consumers for
whom durability, good battery life and price are most important. The Mobile Phones business unit also produced a range of affordable smartphones whose hardware, software and services are optimized for&#151;and not compromised by&#151;low price
points. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As part of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Microsoft has licensed, for a limited time, the Nokia brand for use with certain products from the Mobile
Phones unit as well as acquired the Asha brand
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
under which Nokia marketed some of its Mobile Phones products. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Smart Devices </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Smart Devices unit, which was acquired by Microsoft in its entirety, focused on developing smartphones based on Microsoft&#146;s Windows Phone operating system.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia brought its first Windows Phone products&#151;under the Lumia brand&#151;to market in 2011, several months after entering into a strategic partnership with
Microsoft. We then expanded the depth and breadth of the Lumia range, contributing to Windows Phone becoming the third-largest ecosystem globally in 2013. As part of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Microsoft acquired the Lumia brand under which
Microsoft has continued to introduce new smartphone models, following the completion of the transaction. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Sales and marketing </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We derived our net sales of mobile devices primarily from sales to mobile network operators and distributors, and to a lesser extent independent retailers, corporate
customers and consumers. The marketing efforts were aimed at creating consumer loyalty, enhancing the Nokia brand and increasing sales. In the marketing of mobile devices, we focused on expanding awareness of the key points of differentiation in our
products and services, such as the imaging capabilities of our high-end smartphones or the robust quality and long battery life of our most affordable feature phones. We also engaged consumers through our own social media channels, including Nokia
Conversations. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Business overview </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Production of mobile devices </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Until the closing of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Nokia operated a total of eight production facilities with the purpose of production and customization of mobile
devices. The production facilities were located in Manaus, Brazil; Beijing and Dongguan, China; Kom&aacute;rom, Hungary; Chennai, India; Reynosa, Mexico; Masan, Republic of Korea and Hanoi, Vietnam. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Following the closing of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Nokia&#146;s manufacturing facilities transferred to Microsoft with the exception of the manufacturing
facilities located in Chennai, India and Masan, Republic of Korea. In India, our manufacturing facility is subject to an asset freeze by the Indian tax authorities as a result of ongoing tax proceedings and was not transferred to Microsoft as part
of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. It continued to produce devices under contract for Microsoft following the close of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, however, production was suspended in November 2014 due to a lack of orders. In the Republic of
Korea, Nokia and Microsoft agreed to exclude the Masan facility from the scope of the transaction, resulting in Nokia closing the site in 2014. None of these facilities is subject to a material encumbrance, with the exception of the mobile devices
related facility in Chennai as tax authorities in India have placed a lien on this facility prohibiting Nokia to transfer this facility.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
</DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:20pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_16"></A><A NAME="tx861743_55"></A>Principal industry trends affecting operations </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_09g"></A>Business specific trends </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Nokia Networks </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks is a leading vendor in the mobile
infrastructure market, providing a broad range of different products, from the hardware components of networks used by network operators to software solutions supporting the efficient interaction of networks, as well as services to plan, optimize,
implement, run and upgrade mobile operators&#146; networks. Nokia Networks is investing in the innovative products and services needed by telecom operators to manage the increase in wireless data traffic. Nokia Networks plans to focus its future
investments in further building on its strong position in mobile broadband and related services, and strengthening its leadership position in next-generation network technologies, which it believes will be important enablers for the future networks
by connecting tens of billions of devices. For more information on the Nokia Networks business refer to &#147;Business overview&#151;Nokia Networks&#148; above. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Industry trends </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In recent years, the most important trends affecting Nokia
Networks have been the increase in the use of mobile data services and the resulting exponential increase in data traffic, which has resulted in an increased need for high performance, quality and reliability in networks. The continuing data traffic
increase has, however, not been directly reflected in operators&#146; revenue. As a result, there is an increased need for efficiency for both operators and network infrastructure and services vendors. In addition to the attempts to reduce their
costs, the operators want to increase their agility through the adoption of the emerging Telco Cloud and network virtualization technologies. Operator consolidation driven by operators&#146; needs to provide a wider spectrum of services, especially
fixed-mobile convergence services, is also a key trend that affects Nokia Networks&#146; business. For more information, refer to &#147;Business overview&#151;Nokia Networks&#148;. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Pricing and price erosion </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The pricing
environment was challenging in 2014, with competition remaining intense and the consequent price erosion impacting Nokia Networks&#146; net sales and profitability. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Product mix </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks&#146; profitability
is also impacted by product mix including the share of software in the sales mix. Products and services have varying profitability profiles. The Mobile Broadband segment offers a combination of hardware and software. Software products generally have
higher gross margins; however, they require significantly higher R&amp;D investments. Global Services&#146; offerings are typically labor-intensive, while carrying low R&amp;D investment, and have relatively low gross margins compared to the
hardware and software products of Mobile Broadband. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Seasonality and cyclical nature of projects </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks&#146; sales are affected by seasonality in the network operators&#146; spending cycles, with generally higher sales in the fourth quarter, as compared to
the first quarter of the following year. In addition to normal industry seasonality, there are normal peaks and troughs in the deployment of large infrastructure projects. The timing of these projects is dependent on new radio spectrum allocation,
network upgrade cycles and the availability of new consumer devices, which in turn affects Nokia Networks&#146; sales. Nokia Networks&#146; profitability can be affected by changes in the sales volume, as well as the requirement to source large
volumes of components on short notice, which can impact the
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
cost of sales, or in cases where component shortages emerge, the net sales. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Continued operational efficiency improvements </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, the transformation to an efficient operating model brought industry leading margins and increased R&amp;D efficiency to Nokia Networks. Nokia Networks
continues to focus on operational improvement across its business. Its &#147;Smarter&#148; program has a key role in making Nokia Networks a continuously more efficient and high-performing company positioned for long-term success. The program aims
at further strengthening its productivity, efficiency and competitive cost structure, and Nokia Networks will bring performance excellence methodologies such as Kaizen, Lean, and Six Sigma to every part of its business. Nokia Networks also pursues
further efficiency gains from increased automation in Global Services delivery and in other areas, as well as continued improvements in R&amp;D efficiency and agility. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:30pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Uninterrupted service even at the biggest events </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Nokia Smart Scheduler, used to optimize cell capacity and network efficiency, played a key part in delivering seamless LTE services at one of the world&#146;s
showcase racing events in Shanghai. By managing uplink interference, signalling capacity and load balancing, it delivered uninterrupted service availability throughout the event.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<TR>
<TD WIDTH="51%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="47%"></TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom">NOKIA ANNUAL REPORT ON FORM 20-F 2014</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" ALIGN="right">42</TD></TR></TABLE>

<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_15"></A>Operating and financial review and prospects </P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Cost of components and raw materials </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">There are several factors driving Nokia Networks&#146; profitability: scale, operational efficiency, and pricing and cost discipline have been, and will continue to be,
important factors affecting profitability and competitiveness. Nokia Networks product costs comprise, among others, components, manufacturing, labor and overhead, royalties and licensing fees, depreciation of product machinery, logistics and
warranty and other quality costs. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Targets and priorities </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Over the long term, Nokia targets to grow Nokia Networks&#146; net sales slightly faster than the market. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia expects Nokia Networks&#146; operating margin for the full year 2015 to be in-line with Nokia Networks&#146; targeted long-term operating margin range of 8% to
11%, excluding special items and purchase price accounting related items. In addition, Nokia expects Nokia Networks&#146; net sales to grow on a year-on-year basis for the full year 2015. This outlook is based on Nokia&#146;s expectations regarding
a number of factors, including: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="10" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">competitive industry dynamics; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="10" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">product and regional mix; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="10" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">the timing of major new network deployments; and </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="10" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD WIDTH="1" VALIGN="top">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">expected continued improvement under Nokia Networks&#146; transformation programs.
</TD></TR></TABLE></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>HERE </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our HERE business is a
leading provider of maps and location experiences across multiple screens and operating systems and is focused on producing the most accurate and fresh map content, available across multiple devices and operating systems. HERE continues to invest in
its leading location cloud to make it the source of location intelligence and experiences across many different operating systems, platforms and screens. We believe that location is an essential element of the Programmable World, hence HERE is
targeting its investments in three areas: 1) Automotive, for location content and technology for smart, connected and automated cars; 2) Enterprise, for location-based analytics for better business decisions; and 3) Consumer, for serving ecosystem
players through cloud-based services for personal mobility and location intelligence through seamless experiences across multiple screens. For more information on the HERE business, refer to &#147;Business overview&#151;HERE&#148;. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Seasonality </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE&#146;s sales to the
automotive industry are affected by seasonality in the automobile market, navigation device market, and mobile device market, with generally higher sales in the fourth quarter compared to the first quarter of the following year. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Vertical and horizontal opportunities in map content, platform and applications </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE&#146;S a leading provider of map content and is focused on providing the richest, most accurate and most current maps, since these attributes are becoming more
important for consumers and providers of location services. HERE&#146;s ability to offer independent solutions at various levels of the value chain offers both vertical and horizontal opportunities. HERE sees opportunities in being able to offer
solutions across ecosystems and different layers within them, as well as with different types of devices. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE&#146;s map data provides a strong foundation, and
HERE&#146;s cloud-based map platform enables it to compete at multiple levels of the location services value chain including partnering with automobile vendors and internet services companies for their own differentiated offerings. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:30pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Great maps for the world&#146;s most popular mobile platform </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, HERE launched its first application on Android, featuring accurate maps that are always available, even without an internet connection. Using GPS for location
finding, the app allows users to browse maps, search for places and navigate even when they don&#146;t have access to a network.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Principal industry trends affecting operations continued </P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The HERE map platform enables its location intelligence and experiences to be distributed in the cloud and to be used
across a number of different operating systems, platforms and screens. This enables the users of the platform to license certain parts of HERE&#146;s offering, including content, location services and functionalities, such as routing and
positioning. Access to the platform also allows users to build specific applications for location-related services. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Automotive and other
industries </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We continue to see opportunities within the automotive and other industries that require location-based intelligence. An area of interest is
the automotive industry, where HERE&#146;s focus goes beyond the traditional turn-byturn navigation, with HERE&#146;s offering planned to evolve towards &#147;Smart Guidance&#148;&#151;HERE&#146;s connected driving solutions for inside and outside
of the car&#150; and later towards concepts such as the &#147;Intelligent Car&#148; for automated driving. As these are new areas, our success in terms of transforming development efforts into sales is dependent on these concepts developing
commercially and on HERE&#146;s ability to produce products that are appealing to the industry. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In general, we believe there is a trend of more and more connected
things. We believe there may be opportunities for location-based intelligence to serve as a key dimension in this development, and we believe HERE is well-positioned to capture those potential growth opportunities with its broad offering, for
example through our business with Samsung. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As the automotive industry is a significant contributor to HERE&#146;s sales, the broader economy and its influence on
new car sales are an important factor in HERE&#146;s future success. The adoption rate of in-car navigation products and services is important as well. In general, adoption of these types of products has been increasing and is expected to further
increase as connectivity becomes more pervasive in cars. The gradual move towards an increasing number of cars being connected also offers the possibility to provide more value-adding services to the automotive industry. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Targets and priorities </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia expects
HERE&#146;s net sales to grow on a year-on-year basis for the full year 2015, and HERE&#146;s operating margin for the full year 2015 to be between 7% and 12%, excluding special items and purchase price accounting related items.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Nokia Technologies </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia
Technologies pursues new business opportunities built on Nokia&#146;s innovations and the Nokia brand. Nokia Technologies develops and licenses cutting-edge innovations that are powering the next revolution in computing and mobility. The Nokia
Technologies strategy consists of: 1) Patent Licensing, focused on licensing our standard-essential and other patents to companies in the mobile devices market, consumer electronics and beyond; 2) Technology Licensing, focused on licensing and
transferring proprietary technologies to enable our partners to build better products; 3) Brand Licensing, to help our customers leverage the value of the Nokia brand in consumer devices; and 4) Incubation, focused on developing new ideas and
prototypes; all supported by Nokia Labs, our world-class R&amp;D team. For more information on the Nokia Technologies business, refer to &#147;Business overview&#151;Nokia Technologies&#148;. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Monetization strategies of IPR </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Success in the
technology industry requires significant R&amp;D investments, with the resulting patents and other IPR utilized to protect and generate a return on those investments and related inventions. In recent years, we have seen new entrants in the mobile
device industry, many of which do not have licenses to our patents. Our aim is to approach these companies by potentially utilizing one or more means of monetization. We believe we are well-positioned to protect, and build on, our existing
industry-leading patent portfolio, and consequently increasing shareholder value. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We see three main means for monetizing our innovations: 1) Patent Licensing; 2)
Technology Licensing; and 3) Brand Licensing. Additionally, our incubation activities may from time to time lead to concepts that we could consider licensing or bringing to the market as products or services ourselves. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In Patent Licensing, the main opportunities we are pursuing are: 1) renewal of existing license agreements, and negotiating new license agreements with mobile device
manufacturers; and 2) expanding the scope of licensing activities to other industries, in particular those that implement mobile communication technologies. We no longer need patent licenses for our former Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business,
enabling possibilities to improve the balance of inbound and outbound patent licensing. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:30pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Global service delivery </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks successfully delivers Vodafone Australia multi-technology, multi-vendor Managed Services from its Global Delivery Center in India. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:16pt; font-family:ARIAL">&#147;We believe there may be opportunities for location-based services support the internet of things.&#148;
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review and prospects </P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In Technology Licensing the opportunities are more long term in our view, but we will look at opportunities to license
technologies developed by Nokia Technologies and delivered to partners in consumer electronics as solutions or technology packages that can be integrated into their products and services to help enable the Programmable World. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In Brand Licensing, we will continue to look at further opportunities to bring the Nokia brand into consumer devices, by licensing our brand and other intellectual
property, as well as, for example, industrial design. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">To grow each of the aforementioned business programs it is necessary to invest in commercial capabilities to
support them. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>General trends in IPR licensing </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In general, there has been increased focus on IPR protection and licensing, and this trend is expected to continue. As such, new agreements are generally a product of
lengthy negotiations and potential litigation or arbitration, and therefore the timing and outcome may be difficult to forecast. Due to the structure of the patent license agreements, the payments may be very infrequent, at times retrospective in
part, and the lengths of license agreements can vary. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Additionally, there are clear regional differences in the ease of protecting and licensing patented
innovations. We have seen some licensees actively avoiding license payments, and some licensors using aggressive methods to collect them, both behaviors attracting regulatory attention. We expect the discussion on the regulation of licensing to
continue on both a global and regional level. Some of those regulatory developments may be adverse to the interests of technology developers and patent owners, including Nokia. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Research, development and patent portfolio development </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As the creation of new technology assets and patented innovations is heavily focused on R&amp;D activities with long lead-time to incremental revenues, we may from time
to time see investment opportunities that have strategic importance. This generally affects the operating expenses before sales reflect a return on those investments. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Targets and priorities </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia expects Nokia
Technologies&#146; net sales to grow on a year-on-year basis for the full year 2015, excluding potential amounts related to the expected resolution of the arbitration with Samsung. Also, Nokia expects Nokia Technologies&#146; operating expenses,
excluding special items and purchase price accounting related items, to increase meaningfully on a year-on-year basis for the full year 2015. More specifically, Nokia expects Nokia Technologies&#146; quarterly operating expenses, excluding special
items and purchase price accounting related items, in 2015 to be approximately in-line with the fourth quarter 2014 level. This is related to higher investments in licensing activities, licensable technologies, and business enablers including
go-to-market capabilities, which target new and significant long-term growth opportunities. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_09h"></A>Trends affecting our businesses </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Exchange rates </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia is a company with global operations and net sales
derived from various countries and invoiced in various currencies. Therefore, our business and results from operations are exposed to changes in exchange rates between the euro, our reporting currency, and other currencies, such as the US dollar,
Japanese yen and the Chinese yuan. The magnitude of foreign exchange exposures changes over time as a function of our net sales and costs in different markets, as
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
well as the prevalent currencies used for transactions in those markets. Refer also to &#147;General Facts on Nokia&#151;Selected financial data&#151;Exchange rate data&#148; below. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">To mitigate the impact of changes in exchange rates on our results, we hedge material net foreign exchange exposures (net sales less costs in a currency). We hedge
forecasted net cash flows typically with up to 12-month hedging horizon. For the majority of these hedges, hedge accounting is applied to reduce income statement volatility. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, approximately 30% of Continuing operations net sales and approximately 35% of Nokia&#146;s continuing operations costs were denominated in euro. In 2014,
approximately 30% of Nokia&#146;s continuing operations net sales were denominated in US dollar and approximately 10% each were denominated in Japanese yen, and in Chinese yuan. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">During 2014, the US dollar appreciated against the euro and this had a positive impact on our net sales expressed in euros. However, the stronger US dollar also
contributed to higher cost of sales and operating expenses, as approximately 30% of our total cost base was in US dollars. In total, before hedging, the appreciation of the US dollar had a small positive effect on our operating profit in 2014. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">During 2014, the Japanese yen depreciated against the euro and this had a negative impact on our net sales expressed in euros. However, the weaker Japanese yen also
contributed to lower cost of sales and operating expenses, as approximately 5% of Nokia&#146;s continuing operations total costs were denominated in Japanese yen. In total, before hedging, the depreciation of the Japanese yen had a small negative
effect on our operating profit in 2014. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">During 2014, the Chinese yuan appreciated against the euro and this had a positive impact on our net sales expressed in
euros. However, the stronger Chinese yuan also contributed to higher cost of sales and operating expenses, as approximately 10% of Nokia&#146;s continuing operations total costs were denominated in Chinese yuan. In total, before hedging, the
appreciation of the Chinese yuan had a small negative effect on our operating profit in 2014. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Significant changes in exchange rates may also impact our competitive
position and related price pressures through their impact on our competitors. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">For a discussion on the instruments used by Nokia in connection with our hedging
activities, refer to Note 35, Risk management, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. Refer also to &#147;Operating and financial review and prospects&#151;Risk Factors&#151;Risks relating to
Nokia&#148;. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:20pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_56"></A><A NAME="tx861743_18"></A>Results of operations </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The financial data included in this &#147;Operating and financial review and prospects&#148; section at and for the year
ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 and 2014 and for each of the years in the three-year period ended December&nbsp;31, 2014 has been derived from our audited consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. The financial data
at December</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
31, 2013 and 2014 and for each of the years in the three-year period ended December&nbsp;31, 2014 should be read in conjunction with, and are qualified in their entirety by reference to, our
audited consolidated financial statements. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_57"></A>Continuing operations </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2014 compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth selective line items and the percentage of net sales that they represent for the years indicated. </P>

<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Continuing operations&#146; net sales in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;12&nbsp;732&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;23&nbsp;million compared to
EUR&nbsp;12&nbsp;709&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in Continuing operations&#146; net sales was primarily attributable to higher net sales in HERE and Nokia Technologies. The increase was partially offset by a slight decrease in net sales in
Nokia Networks. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The increase in net sales in HERE was primarily attributable to higher sales to vehicle customers and Microsoft becoming a more significant
licensee of HERE&#146;s services. The increase in net sales in Nokia Technologies was primarily attributable to higher intellectual property licensing income from certain licensees, including Microsoft becoming a more significant intellectual
property licensee in conjunction with the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. The slight decrease in net sales in Nokia Networks was primarily attributable to a decrease in net sales in Global Services, as well as the absence of sales from businesses that
were divested and certain customer contracts and countries that were exited in 2013. The decrease in net sales in Global Services was partially offset by an increase in net sales in Mobile Broadband.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">

for the main changes in the regional net sales. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Gross margin for Continuing operations in 2014 was 44.3% compared to 42.1% in 2013. The increase in Continuing operations gross margin was primarily attributable to the
increase in gross margin in Nokia Networks. The increase was partially offset by a decrease in gross margin in HERE. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The increase in gross margin in Nokia Networks
was primarily attributable to a higher proportion of Mobile Broadband in the overall sales mix and an increase in gross margin in Global Services was partially offset by a slight decrease in gross margin in Mobile Broadband. The decrease in gross
margin in HERE was primarily due to certain ongoing expenses that had been previously borne by our former Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business.

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_17"></A>Operating and financial review </P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our R&amp;D expenses for Continuing operations in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;493&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;126&nbsp;million, or 5%, compared to
EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;619&nbsp;million in 2013. R&amp;D expenses represented 19.6% of our net sales in 2014 compared to 20.6% in 2013. The decrease in R&amp;D expenses was primarily attributable to the decrease in R&amp;D expenses in HERE and Nokia
Networks, partially offset by higher R&amp;D expenses in Nokia Technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The decrease in R&amp;D expenses in HERE was primarily attributable to significant
purchase price accounting related items of EUR&nbsp;168&nbsp;million in 2013 arising from the purchase of NAVTEQ, the majority of which were fully amortized in 2013. The decrease in R&amp;D expenses in Nokia Networks was primarily attributable to
lower subcontracting costs, partially offset by higher investments in targeted growth areas, most notably LTE, small cells and Telco Cloud. The increase in R&amp;D expenses in Nokia Technologies was primarily attributable to investments in business
activities, such as the Technology and Brand licensing opportunities, which target new and significant long-term growth opportunities. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">R&amp;D expenses included
purchase price accounting related items of EUR&nbsp;36&nbsp;million in 2014 compared to EUR&nbsp;188&nbsp;million in 2013. In 2014, R&amp;D expenses included EUR&nbsp;23&nbsp;million of transaction related personnel costs related to the Sale of the
D&amp;S Business compared to EUR&nbsp;15&nbsp;million in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our selling, general and administrative expenses for Continuing operations in 2014 were
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;634&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;37&nbsp;million, or 2%, compared to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;671&nbsp;million in 2013. Selling, general and administrative expenses represented 12.8% of our net sales in 2014 compared to 13.1% in 2013.
The decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses was primarily attributable to the decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses in Nokia Networks. The decrease was partially offset by an increase in selling, general and
administrative expenses in Group Common Functions and Nokia Technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses in Nokia Networks was
primarily attributable to structural cost savings from Nokia Networks&#146; global restructuring program. The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses in Group Common Functions was primarily attributable to transaction related costs
resulting from the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses in Nokia Technologies was primarily attributable to increased activities related to anticipated and ongoing patent licensing cases. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Selling, general and administrative expenses included purchase price accounting items of EUR&nbsp;40&nbsp;million in 2014 compared to EUR&nbsp;93&nbsp;million in 2013.
In 2014, selling, general and administrative expenses included EUR&nbsp;31&nbsp;million of transaction related costs related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">A goodwill impairment charge of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;209&nbsp;million was recorded in the third quarter 2014. Refer to Note
10, Impairment, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Other income and expenses for Continuing operations in 2014
was a net expense of EUR&nbsp;132&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;404&nbsp;million, or 75%, compared to a net expense of EUR&nbsp;536&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease in other income and expenses was primarily attributable to lower
restructuring and associated charges at Nokia Networks, partially offset by lower other income in Group Common Functions. In 2014, other income and expenses included restructuring and associated charges of EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million at Nokia Networks,
charges related to the HERE cost reduction program of EUR&nbsp;36&nbsp;million and anticipated contractual remediation costs of EUR&nbsp;31&nbsp;million at Nokia Networks. In 2013, other income and expenses included restructuring and associated
charges of EUR&nbsp;602&nbsp;million, and transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;18&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our operating profit for
Continuing operations in 2014 was EUR&nbsp;170&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;349&nbsp;million, or 67%, compared to an operating profit of EUR&nbsp;519&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease in operating profit was primarily attributable to the
goodwill impairment charge relating to HERE and the higher expenses in Group Common Functions. The decrease was partially offset by an increase in operating profit in Nokia Networks and Nokia Technologies. Our operating profit in 2014 included a
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;209&nbsp;million goodwill impairment charge relating to HERE. In addition, it included purchase price accounting items, restructuring charges and other special items of EUR&nbsp;253&nbsp;million compared to EUR&nbsp;917&nbsp;million
in 2013. Our operating margin in 2014 was 1.3% compared to 4.1% in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net financial income and expenses </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Financial income and expenses for Continuing operations was a net expense of EUR&nbsp;395&nbsp;million in 2014 compared to a net expense of EUR&nbsp;280&nbsp;million in
2013, an increase of EUR&nbsp;115&nbsp;million, or 41%. The higher net financial expense in 2014 was primarily attributable to EUR&nbsp;123&nbsp;million of one-time expense related to the redemption of materially all of Nokia Networks&#146;
borrowings, and a non-cash charge of EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million related to the repayment of EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion convertible bonds issued to Microsoft in the second quarter 2014. These charges were partially offset by reduced interest expenses during
the second half of the year and lower net losses related to foreign exchange. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Refer to &#147;&#151;Liquidity and capital resources&#148; below.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Results of operations continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Profit/loss before taxes </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Continuing operations loss before tax in 2014 was a loss of EUR&nbsp;237&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;480&nbsp;million compared to a profit before tax of
EUR&nbsp;243&nbsp;million in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Income tax </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Income taxes for Continuing operations amounted to a net benefit of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;408&nbsp;million in 2014, a change of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;610&nbsp;million compared to a
net expense of EUR&nbsp;202&nbsp;million in 2013. The net income tax benefit was primarily attributable to the recognition of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;126&nbsp;million of deferred tax assets from the reassessment of recoverability of tax assets in Finland
and Germany in 2014. This resulted in a EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;034&nbsp;million non-cash tax benefit in the third quarter 2014. Based on recent profitability and forecasts at that time, we were able to re-establish a pattern of sufficient profitability in
Finland and Germany to utilize the cumulative losses, foreign tax credits and other temporary differences. A significant portion of our Finnish and German deferred tax assets are indefinite in nature and available against future Finnish and German
tax liabilities. The EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;034&nbsp;million non-cash tax benefit was partially offset by the recognition of a net expense of EUR&nbsp;341&nbsp;million in valuation allowances related to HERE&#146;s Dutch deferred tax assets in 2014. Refer
to Note 13, Income Tax and Note 14, Deferred taxes, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our current tax for
Continuing operations was an income tax expense of EUR&nbsp;374&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2014, compared to EUR&nbsp;354&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013. Refer to Note 13, Income taxes, of our
consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Non-controlling interests </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Profit for Continuing operations attributable to non-controlling interests in 2014 was EUR&nbsp;8&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;153&nbsp;million compared to a
loss attributable to non-controlling interests of EUR&nbsp;145&nbsp;million for 2013. The change was primarily attributable to our acquisition of Siemens&#146; stake in Nokia Networks (formerly Nokia Siemens Networks) in August 2013, which
significantly reduced the non-controlling interests in that business. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Profit/loss attributable to equity holders of the parent and
earnings per share </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Group&#146;s total profit attributable to equity holders of the parent in 2014 was EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;462&nbsp;million, an
increase of EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;077&nbsp;million, compared to a loss of EUR&nbsp;615&nbsp;million in 2013. This included a gain of EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;175&nbsp;million from the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. Continuing operations generated a profit
attributable to equity holders of the parent in 2014, amounting to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;163&nbsp;million compared to a profit of EUR&nbsp;186&nbsp;million in 2013. Nokia Group&#146;s total basic earnings per share in 2014 increased to EUR&nbsp;0.94
(basic) and EUR&nbsp;0.85 (diluted) compared to EUR&nbsp;(0.17) (basic) and EUR&nbsp;(0.17) (diluted) in 2013. From Continuing operations, earnings per share in 2014 increased to EUR&nbsp;0.31 (basic) and EUR&nbsp;0.30 (diluted) compared to
EUR&nbsp;0.05 (basic) and EUR&nbsp;0.05 (diluted) in 2013. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 </B></P>

<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Continuing operations&#146; net sales declined by 17% to EUR&nbsp;12&nbsp;709&nbsp;million in 2013 compared with EUR&nbsp;15&nbsp;400&nbsp;million in 2012. The decline
in Continuing operations net sales in 2013 was primarily due to lower Nokia Networks and HERE net sales. The decline in Nokia Networks&#146; net sales was partially due to divestments of businesses not consistent with its strategic focus, as well as
the exiting of certain customer contracts and countries. Excluding these two factors, Nokia Networks&#146; net sales in 2013 declined by approximately 13% primarily due to reduced wireless infrastructure deployment activity, which affected both
Global Services and Mobile Broadband. The decline in HERE net sales was primarily due to a decline in internal* HERE net sales due to lower recognition of deferred revenue related to our smartphone sales, partially offset by an increase in external
HERE net sales due to higher sales to vehicle customers. Additionally, Nokia Networks&#146; and HERE net sales were adversely affected by foreign currency fluctuations. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth the distribution by geographical area of our net sales for the fiscal years 2013 and 2012. </P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">All Nokia Technologies net sales are allocated to Finland. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">*</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">HERE internal sales refers to sales that HERE had to our Discontinued operations (formerly Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business) that used certain HERE services in its mobile devices. After the closing of the Sale of
the D&amp;S Business, HERE no longer generates such internal sales, however, it will continue to recognize deferred revenue related to this business for up to 24 months after the closing of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. As part of the Sale of
the D&amp;S Business, Microsoft will become a strategic licensee of the HERE platform, and will separately pay HERE for a four-year license that will be recognized ratably as external net sales.
</TD></TR></TABLE></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Gross margin for Continuing operations in 2013 was 42.1%, compared to 36.1% in 2012. The increase in 2013 was primarily due to a higher Nokia Networks&#146; gross
margin. Nokia Networks&#146; gross margin increased primarily due to improved efficiency in Global Services, an improved product mix with a greater share of higher margin products, and the divestment of less profitable businesses. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our R&amp;D expenses were
EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;619&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;081&nbsp;million in 2012. R&amp;D expenses represented 20.6% of our net sales in 2013, compared to 20.0% in 2012. R&amp;D expenses included purchase price accounting items of
EUR&nbsp;188&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;375&nbsp;million in 2012. The decrease was primarily due to lower amortization of acquired intangible assets within HERE. In addition, it included EUR&nbsp;15&nbsp;million of transaction
related costs, related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, our selling and marketing expenses were EUR&nbsp;974&nbsp;million, compared to
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;372&nbsp;million in 2012. Selling and marketing expenses represented 7.7% of our net sales in 2013 compared to 8.9% in 2012. The decrease in selling and marketing expenses was due to lower purchase price accounting items and
generally lower expenses in Nokia Networks and HERE. Selling and marketing expenses included purchase price accounting items of EUR&nbsp;93&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;313&nbsp;million in 2012. The decrease was primarily due to items
arising from the formation of Nokia Networks becoming fully amortized at the end of the first quarter of 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Administrative and general expenses were
EUR&nbsp;697&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;690&nbsp;million in 2012. Administrative and general expenses were equal to 5.5% of our net sales in 2013 compared to 4.5% in 2012. The increase in administrative and general expenses as a
percentage of net sales reflected a decline in net sales in 2013. Administrative and general expenses did not include purchase price accounting items in either 2013 or 2012. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Other income and expenses was a net expense of EUR&nbsp;536&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to a net expense of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;237&nbsp;million in 2012. In 2013, other
income and expenses included restructuring charges of EUR&nbsp;602&nbsp;million, as well as transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;18&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. In 2012, other income and expenses included restructuring
charges of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;265&nbsp;million, including EUR&nbsp;42&nbsp;million related to country and contract exits, impairments of assets of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;million, a negative adjustment of EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;million to purchase price allocations
related to the final payment from Motorola as well as amortization of acquired intangible assets of EUR&nbsp;23&nbsp;million and a net gain on sale of real estate of EUR&nbsp;79 million.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<TR>
<TD WIDTH="51%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="47%"></TD></TR>

<TD VALIGN="bottom">NOKIA ANNUAL REPORT ON FORM 20-F 2014</TD>

<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Results of operations continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our 2013 operating profit was EUR&nbsp;519&nbsp;million, compared with an operating loss of EUR&nbsp;821&nbsp;million in 2012. The increased operating profit resulted
primarily from lower restructuring charges and purchase price accounting items in general and an increase in the operating performance of our Nokia Networks and HERE businesses. Our operating profit in 2013 included purchase price accounting items,
restructuring charges and other special items of net negative EUR&nbsp;917&nbsp;million compared to net negative EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;963&nbsp;million in 2012. Our 2013 operating margin was positive 4.1% compared to negative 5.3% in 2012. The improvement
was primarily due to an increase in our gross margin and lower expenses in other income and expenses. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net financial income and expenses
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Financial income and expenses, net, was an expense of EUR&nbsp;280&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to an expense of EUR&nbsp;357&nbsp;million in 2012. The
lower net expense in 2013 was primarily driven by lower foreign exchange losses. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Refer&#151;&#147;Liquidity and capital resources&#148; below.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Profit/loss before taxes </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Continuing operations&#146; profit before tax was EUR&nbsp;243&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to a loss of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;179&nbsp;million in 2012. Taxes amounted to
EUR&nbsp;202&nbsp;million in 2013 and EUR&nbsp;304&nbsp;million in 2012. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Non-controlling interests </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Loss attributable to non-controlling interests from Continuing operations totaled EUR&nbsp;145&nbsp;million in 2013, compared with a loss attributable to
non-controlling interests of EUR&nbsp;712&nbsp;million in 2012. This change was primarily due to an improvement in Nokia Networks results and our acquisition of Siemens&#146; stake in Nokia Networks. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Profit/loss attributable to equity holders of the parent and earnings per share </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Group&#146;s total loss attributable to equity holders of the parent in 2013 amounted to EUR&nbsp;615&nbsp;million, compared with a loss of
EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;105&nbsp;million in 2012. Continuing operations generated a profit attributable to equity holders of the parent in 2013, amounting to EUR&nbsp;186&nbsp;million, compared with a loss of EUR&nbsp;771&nbsp;million in 2012. Nokia
Group&#146;s total earnings per share in 2013 increased to EUR&nbsp;(0.17) (basic) and EUR&nbsp;(0.17) (diluted), compared with EUR&nbsp;(0.84) (basic) and EUR&nbsp;(0.84) (diluted) in 2012. From Continuing operations, earnings per share in 2013
increased to EUR&nbsp;0.05 (basic) and EUR&nbsp;0.05 (diluted), compared with EUR&nbsp;(0.21) (basic) and EUR&nbsp;(0.21) (diluted) in 2012.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(1)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Represents the results of the Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business through to April&nbsp;25, 2014, the gain on the Sale of the D&amp;S Business and subsequent wind-down activities. Refer to Note 3, Disposals treated as
discontinued operations, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations net sales for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014 were EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;458&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;8&nbsp;277&nbsp;million, or
77%, compared to EUR&nbsp;10&nbsp;735&nbsp;million in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations gross margin decreased to 15.1% for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014 compared to a gross margin of 20.6% in 2013. The decrease in gross
margin was primarily attributable to a decrease in gross margin in both Smart Devices and Mobile Phones. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations operating expenses were EUR&nbsp;908&nbsp;million for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014, a decrease of
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;891&nbsp;million, or 68%, compared to EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;799&nbsp;million in 2013. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt; margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations operating profit for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014 was EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;639&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;229&nbsp;million,
compared to an operating loss of EUR&nbsp;590&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in discontinued operations operating profit in 2014 was primarily attributable to the gain of EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;175&nbsp;million from the Sale of the D&amp;S Business.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Profit/(loss) for the period </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued
operations profit for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014 was EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;305&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;085&nbsp;million compared to a loss of EUR&nbsp;780&nbsp;million in 2013. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The increase in Discontinued operations profit for the period ended April&nbsp;25, 2014 was primarily attributable to the gain of EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;175&nbsp;million from
the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. The increase was partially offset by a tax expense of EUR&nbsp;127&nbsp;million primarily due to non-resident capital gains taxes in certain jurisdictions, as well as tax impacts of legal entity restructuring
carried out in connection with the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. Refer to Note 3, Disposals treated as discontinued operations, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>(60</B></TD>

<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations net sales decreased by 29% to EUR&nbsp;10&nbsp;735&nbsp;million compared to EUR&nbsp;15&nbsp;152&nbsp;million in 2012. The decline in
discontinued operations net sales in 2013 was primarily due to lower Mobile Phones net sales and, to a lesser extent, lower Smart Devices net sales. The decline in Mobile Phones net sales was due to lower volumes and average selling prices, affected
by competitive industry dynamics, including intense smartphone competition at increasingly lower price points and intense competition at the low end of our product portfolio. The decline in Smart Devices net sales was primarily due to lower volumes,
affected by competitive industry dynamics, including the strong momentum of competing smartphone platforms, as well as our portfolio transition from Symbian products to Lumia products. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth the distribution by geographical area of our net sales for the fiscal years 2013 and 2012. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="93%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000"><B>For the year ended December 31</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" COLSPAN="2" ALIGN="right" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000"><B>2013</B></TD>
