<P STYLE="font-size:6pt; margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations gross margin improved to 20.6% in 2013 compared to 18.7% in 2012. The increase in gross margin in 2013 was primarily due to a higher Smart
Devices gross margin, partially offset by slightly lower Mobile Phones gross margin. The increase in Smart Devices gross margin was primarily due to lower inventory related allowances, which adversely affected Smart Devices gross margin in 2012.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued
operations operating expenses were EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;799&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;311&nbsp;million in 2012. The 35% decrease in 2013 was due to lower Mobile Phones and Smart Devices operating expenses, primarily due to
structural cost savings, as well as overall cost controls. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Discontinued operations operating margin improved to negative 5.5% in 2013 compared to negative 9.8% in 2012. The improvement was primarily due to structural cost
savings, as well as overall cost controls, and a higher gross margin. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Results of segments continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks&#146; net sales in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;11&nbsp;198&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;84&nbsp;million, or 1%, compared to
EUR&nbsp;11&nbsp;282&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease in Nokia Networks net sales was primarily attributable to a decrease in Global Services net sales, and the absence of sales from businesses that were divested and certain customer contracts and
countries that were exited in 2013. The decrease was partly offset by an increase in Mobile Broadband net sales. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mobile Broadband net sales increased to
EUR&nbsp;6&nbsp;039&nbsp;million in 2014, or 13% compared to EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;347&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase was primarily attributable to an increase in net sales in radio and core networking technologies. The increase in radio technologies
net sales was primarily attributable to growth in LTE. The increase was partially offset by a decrease in net sales in mature radio technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Global Services
net sales decreased to EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;105&nbsp;million in 2014, or 11%, compared to EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;753&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to decreases in net sales in network implementation, managed services including
the exiting of certain customer contracts and countries, as well as a decrease in the care business line. The decrease was partially offset by an increase in net sales in the systems integration business line. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth the distribution by geographical area of our net sales for the years indicated. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="93%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>For the year ended December 31</B></TD>

<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>

<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>(1</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>)&nbsp;</B></TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks net sales in Latin America decreased 20% in 2014 compared to 2013 primarily due to the exiting of certain customer
contracts and lower network deployments in Brazil, Chile and Mexico. In Europe, net sales decreased 4% primarily due to lower network deployments in Western Europe, partially offset by higher network deployments in Eastern Europe. In Asia-Pacific,
net sales decreased 2% primarily due to lower network deployments in Japan, partially offset by higher network deployments in India and Korea. In the Middle East and Africa, net sales decreased 5% primarily due to lower network deployments. In
Greater China, net sales increased 16% primarily due to higher LTE network deployments. In North America, net sales increased 15% primarily due to LTE network deployments at major customers.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks gross margin in 2014 was 38.7%, compared to 36.6% in 2013. The increase in Nokia Networks gross margin was primarily attributable to a higher proportion
of Mobile Broadband in the overall sales mix and an increase in the gross margin of Global Services, partly offset by a slight decrease in the gross margin of Mobile Broadband. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The decrease in the gross margin of Mobile Broadband was primarily attributable to a lower gross margin in mature radio technologies. The decrease was partially offset
by a higher gross margin in LTE and core networking technologies. In addition, Mobile Broadband gross margin in 2014 benefitted from lower costs incurred in anticipation of a technology shift to TD-LTE, which adversely affected the gross margin of
Mobile Broadband in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The increase in the gross margin of Global Services was primarily attributable to a more favorable sales mix including a lower proportion
of managed services and a higher proportion of systems integration in the sales mix, as well as margin improvement in systems integration. The increase was partially offset by lower gross margin in care, network implementation and network planning
and optimization. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks R&amp;D expenses were EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;786&nbsp;million in 2014, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;36&nbsp;million, or 2%, compared to
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;822&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to lower subcontracting costs. The decrease was partially offset by headcount increases mainly related to increased in-house activities. Nokia Networks continues to
invest in targeted growth areas, most notably LTE, small cells and Telco Cloud, while reducing investments in mature technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks selling, general
and administrative expenses were EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;236&nbsp;million in 2014, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;74&nbsp;million, or 6%, compared to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;310&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to structural cost savings from
Nokia Networks&#146; global restructuring program. The decrease was partially offset by headcount increases related to an increased focus on growth. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks
other income and expenses decreased in 2014 to an expense of EUR&nbsp;104&nbsp;million from an expense of EUR&nbsp;582&nbsp;million in 2013. In 2014, other income and expenses included restructuring and associated charges of EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million
and anticipated contractual remediation costs of EUR&nbsp;31 million. In 2013, other income and expenses included restructuring and associated charges of EUR&nbsp;570 million.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks operating profit was EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;210&nbsp;million in 2014, an increase of EUR&nbsp;790&nbsp;million compared to an operating profit of
EUR&nbsp;420&nbsp;million in 2013. Nokia Networks operating margin in 2014 was 10.8% compared to 3.7% in 2013. The increase in operating profit was primarily attributable to an increase in operating profit in Mobile Broadband. The increase was
partially offset by a decrease in operating profit in Global Services. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mobile Broadband operating profit increased from EUR&nbsp;420&nbsp;million in 2013 to
EUR&nbsp;683&nbsp;million in 2014. The increase in operating profit was attributable to higher gross profit. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Global Services operating profit decreased from
EUR&nbsp;693&nbsp;million in 2013 to EUR&nbsp;653&nbsp;million in 2014. The decrease in operating profit was primarily attributable to lower gross profit. The decrease was partially offset by lower operating expenses.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Strategy and restructuring program </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In November 2011, Nokia Networks announced its strategy to focus on mobile broadband and related services, and also launched an extensive global restructuring program,
targeting a reduction of its annualized operating expenses and production overhead, excluding special items and purchase price accounting related items, by EUR&nbsp;1 billion by the end of 2013, compared to the end of 2011. In January 2013, this
target was raised to EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion, and in July 2013 this target was further raised to &#147;more than EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion&#148;. While these savings were expected to come largely from organizational streamlining, the program also targeted
areas such as real estate, information technology, product and service procurement costs, overall general and administrative expenses, and a significant reduction of suppliers in order to further lower costs and improve quality. In 2013, Nokia
Networks achieved its target to reduce operating expenses and production overhead, excluding special items and purchase price accounting items, by more than EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion by the end of 2013, compared to the end of 2011. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia Networks recognized restructuring and associated charges of EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million related to this restructuring program, resulting in cumulative
charges of approximately EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;900&nbsp;million. By the end of 2014, Nokia Networks had cumulative restructuring related cash outflows of approximately EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;550&nbsp;million relating to this restructuring program. Nokia Networks
expects the remaining restructuring related cash outflows relating to this restructuring program to be approximately EUR&nbsp;200&nbsp;million, the majority of which will be paid over the next two years.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>
 <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013
compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth selective line items and the percentage of net sales that they represent for
years indicated. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks Other includes net sales and related cost of sales and operating expenses of non-core businesses, IPR net sales and related
costs, as well as Nokia Networks&#146; Optical business until May&nbsp;6, 2013, when its divestment was completed. It also includes restructuring and associated charges for Nokia Networks business. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks net sales decreased 18% to EUR&nbsp;11&nbsp;282&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;13&nbsp;779&nbsp;million in 2012. The year-on-year decline in
Nokia Networks net sales was primarily due to reduced wireless infrastructure deployment activity affecting both Mobile Broadband and Global Services, as well as the divestments of businesses not consistent with its strategic focus, foreign currency
fluctuations and the exiting of certain customer contracts and countries. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mobile Broadband net sales declined 12% to EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;347&nbsp;million in 2013,
compared to EUR&nbsp;6&nbsp;043&nbsp;million in 2012, as declines in WCDMA, CDMA and GSM were partially offset by growth in both FD-LTE and TD-LTE, reflecting the industry shift to 4G technology. Core network sales declined as a result of the
customer focus on radio technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Global Services net sales declined 17% to EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;753&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to
EUR&nbsp;6&nbsp;929&nbsp;million in 2012 primarily due to the exiting of certain customer contracts and countries as part of Nokia Networks strategy to focus on more profitable business as well as a decline in network roll-outs in Japan and Europe.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth the distribution by geographical area of net sales for the years indicated. </P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:8pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks gross margin was 36.6% in 2013, compared to 30.3% in 2012, driven by improved efficiency in Global Services, an improved product mix with a greater share
of higher margin products, and the divestment of less profitable businesses. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In Mobile Broadband, gross margin improved in 2013 driven by an increased software
share in the product mix, offset by costs incurred in anticipation of a technology shift to TD-LTE. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In Global Services, gross margin improved significantly in 2013
due to the increase in efficiencies as part of our restructuring program and the exit of certain customer contracts and countries as part of Nokia Networks strategy to focus on more profitable business.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks R&amp;D expenses decreased 11% year-on-year in 2013 to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;822&nbsp;million from EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;046&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to
business divestments and reduced investment in business activities not in line with Nokia Networks focused strategy as well as increased R&amp;D efficiency, partially offset by higher investments in business activities that are in line with Nokia
Networks focused strategy, most notably LTE. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks sales and marketing expenses decreased 29% year-on-year in 2013 to EUR&nbsp;821&nbsp;million from
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;158&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to structural cost savings from Nokia Networks restructuring program and a decrease in purchase price accounting related items arising from the formation of Nokia Networks, which were fully
amortized at the end of the first quarter of 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks administrative and general expenses increased 4% year-on-year in 2013 to
EUR&nbsp;489&nbsp;million from EUR&nbsp;470&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to consultancy fees related to finance and information technology related projects, partially offset by structural cost savings. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks other income and expenses decreased in 2013 to an expense of EUR&nbsp;582&nbsp;million from an expense of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;290&nbsp;million in 2012. In
2013, other income and expenses included restructuring charges of EUR&nbsp;570&nbsp;million, including EUR&nbsp;52&nbsp;million related to country and contract exits and EUR&nbsp;157&nbsp;million related to divestments of businesses, and in 2012
included restructuring charges and associated charges of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;226&nbsp;million, including EUR&nbsp;42&nbsp;million related to country and contract exits, divestment of businesses EUR&nbsp;50&nbsp;million, impairment of assets of
EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;million, a negative adjustment of EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;million to purchase price allocations related to the final payment from Motorola, as well as amortization of acquired intangible assets of EUR&nbsp;23 million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Networks operating
profit in 2013 was EUR&nbsp;420&nbsp;million, compared with an operating loss of EUR&nbsp;795&nbsp;million in 2012. Nokia Networks operating margin in 2013 was 3.7%, compared with a negative 5.8% in 2012. The increase in operating profit was
primarily a result of an increase in the contribution of Global Services and a reduction in costs associated with Nokia Networks transformation, consisting mainly of restructuring charges. Further, the purchase price accounting related items arising
from the formation of Nokia Networks were fully amortized at the end of the first quarter of 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The contribution of Mobile Broadband declined from
EUR&nbsp;490&nbsp;million in 2012 to EUR&nbsp;420&nbsp;million in 2013, primarily as a result of lower net sales, which was partially offset by an improved gross margin and a reduction in operating expenses. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The contribution of Global Services increased from EUR&nbsp;334&nbsp;million in 2012 to EUR&nbsp;693&nbsp;million in 2013, as the increase in gross margin more than
compensated for the decline in net sales, and the contribution in 2013 was further supported by a reduction in operating expenses. </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt; margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Strategy and restructuring program </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In November 2011, Nokia Networks announced its strategy to focus on mobile broadband and related services, and also launched an extensive global restructuring program,
targeting a reduction of its annualized operating expenses and production overhead, excluding special items and purchase price accounting related items, by EUR&nbsp;1 billion by the end of 2013, compared to the end of 2011. In January 2013, this
target was raised to EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion, and in July 2013 this target was further raised to &#147;more than EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion&#148;. While these savings were expected to come largely from organizational streamlining, the program also targeted
areas such as real estate, information technology, product and service procurement costs, overall general and administrative expenses, and a significant reduction of suppliers in order to further lower costs and improve quality. In 2013, Nokia
Networks achieved its target to reduce operating expenses and production overhead, excluding special items and purchase price accounting items, by more than EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion by the end of 2013, compared to the end of 2011. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">During 2013, Nokia Networks recognized restructuring charges and other associated items of EUR&nbsp;550&nbsp;million related to this restructuring program, resulting in
cumulative charges of approximately EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;850 million. By the end of 2013, Nokia Networks had cumulative restructuring related cash outflows of approximately EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;250&nbsp;million relating to this restructuring program. Nokia
Networks expects restructuring related cash outflows to be approximately EUR&nbsp;450&nbsp;million for the full year 2014 relating to this restructuring program.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TD VALIGN="top"><B>Net sales</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" ALIGN="right"><B>970</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"></TD>

<P STYLE="font-size:12pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE net sales in 2014 increased EUR&nbsp;56&nbsp;million, or 6%, to EUR&nbsp;970&nbsp;million compared to EUR&nbsp;914&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in HERE net
sales was primarily attributable to higher net sales to vehicle customers and Microsoft becoming a more significant licensee of HERE&#146;s services. The increase was partially offset by lower recognition of revenue to smartphone net sales by our
former Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business and lower net sales to personal navigation device customers, which was consistent with declines in the personal navigation device market. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, HERE had sales of new vehicle licenses of 13.1&nbsp;million units, compared to 10.7&nbsp;million units in 2013, primarily attributable to higher consumer
uptake of in-vehicle navigation and higher vehicle sales. </P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth the distribution by geographical area of net sales for the years indicated. </P>

<p Style='page-break-before:always'>
<HR  SIZE="3" style="COLOR:#999999" WIDTH="100%" ALIGN="CENTER">
<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE gross margin in 2014 was 75.4% compared to 77.2% in 2013. The decrease in HERE gross margin was primarily attributable to certain ongoing expenses that are now
recorded as HERE cost of sales, which were previously recorded as cost of sales by our former Devices&nbsp;&amp; Services business. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE R&amp;D expenses in
2014 were EUR&nbsp;545&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;103&nbsp;million, or 16%, compared to EUR&nbsp;648&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to significant purchase price accounting related items of
EUR&nbsp;168&nbsp;million in 2013 arising from the purchase of NAVTEQ, the majority of which were fully amortized in 2013. The decrease was partially offset by higher investments in targeted growth areas. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE selling, general and administrative expenses were EUR&nbsp;181&nbsp;million in 2014, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;7&nbsp;million, or 4%, compared to
EUR&nbsp;188&nbsp;million in 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to purchase price accounting related items in 2013 arising from the purchase of NAVTEQ, the majority of which were fully amortized in 2013. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">A goodwill impairment charge of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;209&nbsp;million was recorded in the third quarter 2014. The impairment charge was the result of an evaluation of the
projected financial performance and net cash flows resulting in reduced net sales projections. The evaluation incorporated the slower than expected increase in net sales directly to consumers, and our plans to curtail our investment in certain
higher-risk and longer-term growth opportunities. It also reflected the current assessment of risks related to the growth opportunities that we plan to continue pursuing. Refer to Note 10, Impairment, of our consolidated financial statements
included in this annual report on Form 20-F. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE other income and expenses increased in 2014 to a net expense of EUR&nbsp;37&nbsp;million from a net expense of
EUR&nbsp;24&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase was primarily attributable to higher charges related to the cost reduction program.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE operating loss was EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;241&nbsp;million in 2014, an increase of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;087&nbsp;million compared to an operating loss of
EUR&nbsp;154&nbsp;million in 2013. HERE operating margin in 2014 was negative 127.9% compared to negative 16.8% in 2013. The increase in operating loss was primarily attributable to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;209&nbsp;million goodwill impairment charge
recorded in the third quarter 2014. Refer to Note 10, Impairment, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F. The charge was partially offset by the absence of significant purchase price accounting related
items arising from the purchase of NAVTEQ, the majority of which were fully amortized in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Global cost reduction program
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia announced the sharpening of the HERE strategy and an adjustment to the related long-range plan. As part of its decision to curtail
investments in certain higher risk longer term growth opportunities, HERE initiated a cost reduction program during the fourth quarter 2014. Related to this program, HERE recorded charges of approximately EUR&nbsp;36&nbsp;million and had related
cash outflows of approximately EUR&nbsp;12&nbsp;million in 2014. In total, we estimate the cumulative charges will amount to approximately EUR&nbsp;36&nbsp;million and related cash outflows will amount to approximately EUR&nbsp;24 million. Changes
in estimates regarding the timing or amount of costs to be incurred and associated cash flows may become necessary as the program is being completed.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>
 <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013
compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth selective line items and the percentage of net sales that they represent for

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Results of segments continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE net sales decreased 17% to EUR&nbsp;914&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;103&nbsp;million in 2012. HERE internal net sales decreased 59% to
EUR&nbsp;154&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;374&nbsp;million in 2012. HERE external net sales increased 4% to EUR&nbsp;760&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;729&nbsp;million in 2012. The year-on-year decline in HERE internal net
sales was due to lower recognition of deferred revenue related to our smartphone sales. The year-on-year increase in HERE external net sales in 2013 was primarily due to higher sales to vehicle customers, partially offset by lower sales to personal
navigation devices customers. Additionally, HERE net sales were adversely affected by foreign currency fluctuations. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table sets forth HERE net sales
and year-on-year growth rate by geographic area for the fiscal years 2013 and 2012. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="93%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD>
<TD></TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:1pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December 31</B></P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" COLSPAN="2" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" ALIGN="right"><B>2013</B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:1pt; font-size:6.5pt; font-family:ARIAL" align="left"><B>EURm</B></P></TD>

</TABLE> </DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On a year-on-year basis, the decrease in HERE gross margin, 77.2% in 2013 compared to 79.3% in 2012, was primarily due to proportionally higher sales of update units to
vehicle customers, which generally carry a lower gross margin, partially offset by lower costs related to service delivery. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Operating
expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE R&amp;D expenses decreased 27% to EUR&nbsp;648&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;883&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to a
decrease in purchase price accounting related items, EUR&nbsp;168&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;355&nbsp;million in 2012, and cost reduction actions. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE sales and marketing expenses decreased 36% to EUR&nbsp;119&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;186&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily driven by a decrease in
purchase price accounting items, EUR&nbsp;11&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;68&nbsp;million in 2012, cost reduction actions and lower marketing spending. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE administrative and general expenses decreased 10% to EUR&nbsp;69&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;77&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to cost reduction
actions. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, HERE other income and expense had a slightly positive year-on-year impact on profitability, decreasing from EUR&nbsp;30&nbsp;million in 2012 to
EUR&nbsp;24&nbsp;million in 2013. In 2013, we recognized restructuring charges of EUR&nbsp;22&nbsp;million in HERE, compared to EUR&nbsp;31&nbsp;million in 2012. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT
 COLOR="#6d6e71"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">HERE operating loss decreased to EUR&nbsp;154&nbsp;million in 2013, compared with a loss of
EUR&nbsp;301&nbsp;million in 2012. HERE operating margin in 2013 was negative 16.8%, compared with negative 27.3% in 2012. The year-on-year improvement in operating margin in 2013 was driven primarily by the absence of significant purchase price
accounting related items arising from the purchase of NAVTEQ, the vast majority of which had been fully amortized as of the end of the second quarter of 2013.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right">(50</TD>

<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>Operating profit</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>343</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>59.3</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>310</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>58.6</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000" ALIGN="right"><B>11</B></TD>
<TD NOWRAP VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;</B></TD></TR>
</TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies net sales in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;578&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;49&nbsp;million, or 9%, compared to EUR&nbsp;529&nbsp;million in 2013. The
increase in Nokia Technologies net sales was primarily attributable to higher intellectual property licensing income from certain licensees, including Microsoft becoming a more significant intellectual property licensee in conjunction with the Sale
of the D&amp;S Business. The increase was partially offset by decreases in licensing income from certain other licensees that experienced lower levels of business activity, as well as the lower levels of non-recurring IPR income compared to 2013.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies gross
margin in 2014 was 98.6%, compared to 97.4% in 2013. The increase in Nokia Technologies gross margin was primarily attributable to the absence of a one-time cost related to a patent divestment transaction which negatively affected gross margin in
2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia
Technologies R&amp;D expenses in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;161&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;14&nbsp;million, or 10%, compared to EUR&nbsp;147&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in R&amp;D expenses was primarily
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
attributable to investments in business activities, such as building the Technology and Brand licensing units, which target new and significant long-term growth opportunities. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies selling, general and administrative expenses in 2014 were EUR&nbsp;65&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;9&nbsp;million, or 16%, compared to
EUR&nbsp;56&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in selling, general and administrative expenses was primarily attributable to increased activities, such as building the Technology and Brand licensing units, related to anticipated and ongoing patent
licensing cases, as well as higher business support costs. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies other income and expense in 2014 was a net expense of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;million, a
decrease of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;million, or 50%, compared to a net expense of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;million in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies operating profit in 2014 was EUR&nbsp;343&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;33&nbsp;million, or 11%, compared to an operating
profit of EUR&nbsp;310&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in operating profit was primarily attributable to an increase in gross profit. The increase was partially offset by higher operating expenses. Nokia Technologies operating margin in 2014 was
59.3% compared to 58.6% in 2013. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Net sales </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies net sales was stable on a year-on-year basis, EUR&nbsp;529&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;534&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to a
non-recurring license fee of EUR&nbsp;50&nbsp;million in the fourth quarter 2012, partially offset by net increases in royalty payments from our licensees. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT
 COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Gross margin </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On a year-on-year basis, the Nokia Technologies gross margin decreased to 97.4% in 2013 compared to 98.7% in 2012. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating expenses </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies R&amp;D
expenses decreased 4% to EUR&nbsp;147&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;153&nbsp;million in 2012, primarily due to lower R&amp;D costs, partially offset by transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;15&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the
D&amp;S Business. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies sales and marketing expenses increased 42% to EUR&nbsp;34&nbsp;million in 2013 compared to EUR&nbsp;24&nbsp;million in 2012,
primarily due to IPR licensing related litigation expenses. In 2013 sales and marketing expenses included transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies administrative and general expenses were flat year-on-year, amounting to EUR&nbsp;22 million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies other income and expense was approximately flat year-on-year, and included restructuring charges of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to
EUR&nbsp;3&nbsp;million in 2012. </P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Operating profit/loss </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia Technologies operating profit decreased to EUR&nbsp;310&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;325&nbsp;million in 2012. Nokia Technologies operating margin
in 2013 was 58.6%, compared with 60.9% in 2012. The year-on-year decline in operating margin was driven primarily by the transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;17&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business partially offset by decreased
restructuring charges. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_63"></A>Group Common Functions </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2014 compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Group Common Functions operating loss in 2014 was EUR&nbsp;142&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;85&nbsp;million, or 149%, compared to an operating loss of
EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase in operating loss was primarily attributable to the absence of a distribution from an unlisted venture fund related to the disposal of the fund&#146;s investment in Waze Ltd of EUR&nbsp;59&nbsp;million
that benefitted Group Common Functions in 2013. In 2014, Group Common Functions included transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;21&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. In 2013, Group Common Functions included restructuring
charges and associated impairments of EUR&nbsp;10&nbsp;million, as well as transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;18&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 compared to the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Group Common Functions operating loss totaled EUR&nbsp;57&nbsp;million in 2013, compared to EUR&nbsp;50&nbsp;million in 2012. In 2013, Group Common Functions included
restructuring charges and associated impairments of EUR&nbsp;10&nbsp;million, as well as transaction related costs of EUR&nbsp;18&nbsp;million related to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. In 2013, the Group Common Functions benefitted from a
distribution from an unlisted venture fund related to the disposal of the fund&#146;s investment in Waze Ltd of EUR&nbsp;59 million. In 2012, Group Common Functions benefitted from a net gain from sale of real estate of EUR&nbsp;79&nbsp;million and
included restructuring charges of EUR&nbsp;6 million. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:20pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_64">
</A><A NAME="tx861743_20"></A>Liquidity and capital resources </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_65"></A>Financial position </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 Nokia&#146;s total cash and other liquid assets (defined as the aggregate of bank and cash; available-for-sale investments, cash equivalents;
available-for-sale investments, liquid assets; and investments at fair value through profit and loss, liquid assets) equaled EUR&nbsp;7&nbsp;715&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;256&nbsp;million, compared to
EUR&nbsp;8&nbsp;971&nbsp;million at December&nbsp;31, 2013. The decrease was primarily attributable to cash outflows from financing activities related to the repayment of certain debt facilities totaling EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;750&nbsp;million in the first
quarter 2014, as well as the redemption of approximately EUR&nbsp;950&nbsp;million of Nokia Networks debt in the second quarter 2014. Outflows from financing activities were partly offset by the drivers listed below that increased Nokia&#146;s net
cash and other liquid assets. At December&nbsp;31, 2012 Nokia&#146;s total cash and other liquid assets equaled EUR&nbsp;9&nbsp;909 million. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31,
2014 Nokia&#146;s net cash and other liquid assets (defined as total cash and other liquid assets less long-term interest bearing liabilities and short-term borrowings) equaled EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;023&nbsp;million, an increase of
EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;714&nbsp;million, compared to EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;309&nbsp;million at December&nbsp;31, 2013. The increase was primarily attributable to cash proceeds from the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, as well as net cash flow from operating
activities. This increase was partially offset by the execution of the capital structure optimization program, which included payment of a dividend and a special dividend, as well as the repurchase of shares. Nokia&#146;s net cash and other liquid
assets was also adversely impacted by cash outflows related to acquisitions and capital expenditures. At December&nbsp;31, 2012, Nokia&#146;s net cash and other liquid assets equaled EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;360 million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 Nokia&#146;s cash and cash equivalents (defined as the aggregate of bank and cash and available-for-sale investments, cash equivalents)
equaled EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;170&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;463&nbsp;million, compared to EUR&nbsp;7&nbsp;633&nbsp;million at December&nbsp;31, 2013. Nokia&#146;s cash and cash equivalents equaled EUR&nbsp;8&nbsp;952&nbsp;million at
December&nbsp;31, 2012. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_66"></A>Cash flow </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014,
Nokia&#146;s net cash from operating activities equaled EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;275&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;203&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;72&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase was primarily attributable to
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;214&nbsp;million net profit, adjusted for non-cash items and a EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;153&nbsp;million cash release from net working capital. The primary driver for the cash release from net working capital was a
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;650&nbsp;million cash inflow relating to the upfront payment on a ten-year patent license agreement and related option to extend the license into perpetuity with Microsoft, partially offset by approximately EUR&nbsp;320&nbsp;million
restructuring related cash outflows in Continuing operations and approximately EUR&nbsp;210&nbsp;million net working capital related cash outflows in discontinued operations. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia had cash outflows of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;092&nbsp;million related to net financial income and expenses and income taxes, an increase of
EUR&nbsp;935&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;157&nbsp;million in 2013. The increase was primarily attributable to the early redemption of Nokia Networks&#146; borrowings of approximately EUR&nbsp;84&nbsp;million,
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
foreign exchange hedging of approximately EUR&nbsp;180&nbsp;million and income taxes of EUR&nbsp;636&nbsp;million, of which approximately EUR&nbsp;300&nbsp;million were cash outflows relating to
discontinued operations. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, Nokia&#146;s net cash from operating activities equaled EUR&nbsp;72&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;426&nbsp;million, as
compared to EUR&nbsp;354&nbsp;million cash used in operating activities in 2012. The increase was primarily attributable to an increase in profitability and other financial income and expenses, net and a decrease in income taxes paid. The increase
was partially offset by an increase in net working capital cash outflows. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia&#146;s cash flow from investing activities equaled
EUR&nbsp;886&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;577&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;691&nbsp;million cash used in investing activities in 2013. Cash inflows from investing activities was primarily driven by gross proceeds
attributable to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business of approximately EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;010&nbsp;million, which included the proceeds used to repay the convertible bonds issued to Microsoft and the increase in proceeds from maturities and sale of current
available-for-sale investments, liquid assets. The increase was offset by an increase in purchases of current available-for-sale investments, liquid assets. Cash inflows from investing activities also benefitted EUR&nbsp;44&nbsp;million from the
sale of property, plant and equipment. The increase was partially offset by cash outflows related to capital expenditure of EUR&nbsp;311&nbsp;million and acquisitions of EUR&nbsp;175 million. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia&#146;s capital expenditure equaled EUR&nbsp;311&nbsp;million, a decrease of EUR&nbsp;96&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;407&nbsp;million in 2013.
Nokia&#146;s capital expenditure in 2012 equaled EUR&nbsp;461&nbsp;million. Major items of capital expenditure in 2014 included production lines, test equipment and computer hardware used primarily in R&amp;D, office and manufacturing facilities as
well as services and software related intangible assets. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, Nokia&#146;s cash used in investing activities equaled EUR&nbsp;691&nbsp;million, a decrease of
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;253&nbsp;million, as compared to net cash flow from investing activities of EUR&nbsp;562&nbsp;million in 2012. The decrease was primarily attributable to significantly lower proceeds from the sale and maturities of current
available-for-sale investments, liquid assets partially, offset by a decrease in purchases of current available-for-sale investments, liquid assets. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014,
Nokia&#146;s cash flow used in financing activities equaled EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;576&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;4&nbsp;099&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;477&nbsp;million in 2013. Cash outflows from financing activities were primarily
attributable to the repayment of EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;791&nbsp;million in interest-bearing debt, payments of EUR&nbsp;0.11&nbsp;per share in dividend totaling EUR&nbsp;408&nbsp;million and EUR&nbsp;0.26&nbsp;per share in special dividend totaling
EUR&nbsp;966&nbsp;million, as well as EUR&nbsp;427&nbsp;million in cash outflows relating to share repurchases. Nokia also acquired subsidiary shares from a non-controlling interest holder and paid dividends to non-controlling interest holders in
2014 totaling approximately EUR&nbsp;60 million. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Liquidity and capital resources continued </P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2013, Nokia&#146;s cash flow used in financing activities equaled EUR&nbsp;477&nbsp;million, an increase of
EUR&nbsp;12&nbsp;million as compared to EUR&nbsp;465&nbsp;million in 2012. Cash outflows in financing activities were primarily attributable to EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;707&nbsp;million used to purchase the shares in NSN, EUR&nbsp;862&nbsp;million repayment
of long-term borrowings, EUR&nbsp;128&nbsp;million repayment of short-term borrowings and EUR&nbsp;71&nbsp;million payment of dividends, offset by EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;291&nbsp;million in proceeds from long-term borrowings, which included
EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;500&nbsp;million convertible bonds issued to Microsoft and EUR&nbsp;450&nbsp;million and EUR&nbsp;350&nbsp;million bonds due in 2018 and in 2020, respectively, issued by Nokia Networks. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_67"></A>Financial assets and debt </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31,
2014 Nokia&#146;s net cash equaled EUR&nbsp;5&nbsp;023&nbsp;million and consisted of EUR&nbsp;7&nbsp;715&nbsp;million in total cash and other liquid assets and EUR&nbsp;2&nbsp;692&nbsp;million of long-term interest bearing liabilities and short-term
borrowings. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We hold our cash and other liquid assets predominantly in euro. Our liquid assets are mainly invested in high-quality money market and fixed income
instruments with strict maturity limits. Nokia also has a EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;500&nbsp;million undrawn revolving credit facility available for liquidity purposes. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia&#146;s interest-bearing debt consisted of a EUR&nbsp;750&nbsp;million convertible bond due in 2017, a EUR&nbsp;500&nbsp;million bond due in 2019, a USD
1&nbsp;000&nbsp;million bond due in 2019, a USD 500&nbsp;million bond due in 2039 and EUR&nbsp;206&nbsp;million of other liabilities. Refer to Note 35, Risk management, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form
20-F for further information regarding our interest-bearing liabilities. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia repaid a EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;250&nbsp;million bond, a EUR&nbsp;500&nbsp;million
loan from the European Investment Bank (the &#147;EIB&#148;) and EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;500&nbsp;million in convertible bonds issued to Microsoft, which were netted against proceeds from the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. In addition, Nokia prepaid all
material interest-bearing liabilities related to Nokia Networks, including the EUR&nbsp;450&nbsp;million and EUR&nbsp;350&nbsp;million bonds due in 2018 and in 2020, respectively, a EUR&nbsp;88&nbsp;million Finnish pension loan, a
EUR&nbsp;50&nbsp;million loan from the EIB, a EUR&nbsp;16&nbsp;million loan from Nordic Investment Bank and certain other debt. No new debt was issued in 2014. Nokia has no material debt maturing in 2015. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We believe with EUR&nbsp;7&nbsp;715&nbsp;million cash and other liquid assets as well as a EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;500&nbsp;million revolving credit facility, we have
sufficient funds available to satisfy our future working capital needs, capital expenditure, R&amp;D, acquisitions and debt service requirements at least through 2015. We also believe that with our current credit ratings of BB by Standard&nbsp;&amp;
Poor&#146;s and Ba2 by Moody&#146;s, both with positive outlook, we have access to capital markets should any funding needs arise in 2015. Nokia has a target to re-establish its investment grade credit rating. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">There are no material off-balance sheet arrangements that have or are reasonably likely to have a current or future effect on our financial condition, changes in
financial condition, revenues or expenses, results of operations, liquidity, capital expenditures or capital resources that is material to investors.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_68"></A>Capital structure optimization program </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As a result of our significantly improved financial position and earnings profile after closing of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, we announced a EUR&nbsp;5 billion
capital structure optimization program to improve the efficiency of Nokia&#146;s capital structure. The program consists of EUR&nbsp;3 billion of total cash returns to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases and EUR&nbsp;2 billion of
debt reduction by the end of the second quarter 2016. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In accordance with the capital structure optimization program, Nokia paid EUR&nbsp;1&nbsp;374&nbsp;million in
dividends (EUR&nbsp;0.37&nbsp;per share) consisting of EUR&nbsp;408&nbsp;million (EUR&nbsp;0.11&nbsp;per share) of ordinary dividends and EUR&nbsp;966&nbsp;million (EUR&nbsp;0.26&nbsp;per share) of special dividends in 2014. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the EUR&nbsp;1.25 billion share repurchase program, Nokia repurchased 67&nbsp;million shares for EUR&nbsp;427&nbsp;million in 2014. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, Nokia reduced interest bearing debt by approximately EUR&nbsp;950&nbsp;million after the announcement of the capital structure optimization program. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_69"></A>Structured finance </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Structured finance includes
customer financing and other third-party financing. Network operators occasionally require their suppliers, including us, to arrange, facilitate or provide long-term financing as a condition for obtaining infrastructure projects. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 our total customer financing, outstanding and committed equaled EUR&nbsp;156&nbsp;million, an increase of EUR&nbsp;92&nbsp;million, as
compared to EUR&nbsp;64&nbsp;million in 2013. At December&nbsp;31, 2012, our total customer financing, outstanding and committed amounted to EUR&nbsp;108 million. Customer financing primarily consisted of financing commitments to network operators.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Refer to Note 35, Risk management, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F for further information relating to our
committed and outstanding customer financing. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We expect our customer financing commitments to be financed mainly from cash and other liquid assets and through cash
flow from operations. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 guarantees of Nokia&#146;s performance consisted of EUR&nbsp;465&nbsp;million of guarantees that are provided to
certain Nokia Networks&#146; customers in the form of bank guarantees, or corporate guarantees issued by Nokia Networks. These instruments entitle the customer to claim payments as compensation for non-performance by Nokia Networks of its
obligations under network infrastructure supply agreements. Depending on the nature of the instrument, compensation is payable either on demand, or is subject to verification of non-performance.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Operating and financial review </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Financial guarantees and securities pledged that we may give on behalf of customers, represent guarantees relating to
payment by certain Nokia Networks&#146; customers and other third parties under specified loan facilities between such customers or other third parties and their creditors. Nokia&#146;s obligations under such guarantees are released upon the earlier
of expiration of the guarantee or early payment by the customer or other third party. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Refer to Note 30, Commitments and contingencies, of our consolidated
financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F for further information regarding commitments and contingencies. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_70"></A>Venture fund investments and commitments </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">We make
financing commitments to a number of venture funds that make technology related investments. The majority of the investments are managed by Nokia Growth Partners that specializes in growth-stage investing, seeking companies that are changing the
face of mobility and connectivity. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 the fair value of our venture fund investments equaled EUR&nbsp;778&nbsp;million, as compared to
EUR&nbsp;627&nbsp;million at December&nbsp;31, 2013. Refer to note 19, Fair value of financial instruments, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F for further information regarding fair value of our
venture fund investments. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014 our venture fund commitments equaled EUR&nbsp;274&nbsp;million, as compared to EUR&nbsp;215&nbsp;million at
December&nbsp;31, 2013. As a limited partner in venture funds, Nokia is committed to capital contributions and entitled to cash distributions according to the respective partnership agreements and underlying fund activities. Refer to Note 30,
Commitments and contingencies, of our consolidated financial statements included in this annual report on Form 20-F for further information regarding commitments and contingencies.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
</DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:20pt; font-family:ARIAL"><A NAME="tx861743_21"></A><A NAME="tx861743_71"></A>Main events in 2014 </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:18pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; </B></P>
<p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;
</P><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_72"></A>Nokia Corporation and Group highlights </B></P>
<P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">In April 2014, Nokia completed the Sale of the D&amp;S Business. The transaction, which also included an agreement to license patents to Microsoft, was originally announced on September&nbsp;3, 2013. In connection with
the completion of the transaction, Nokia repaid the EUR&nbsp;1.5 billion convertible bonds issued by Nokia to Microsoft. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Following the completion of the transaction, Nokia made a number of announcements, including the following: </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="12" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#150;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The Board appointed Rajeev Suri as President and CEO of Nokia Corporation and the new Nokia Group Leadership Team, effective May&nbsp;1, 2014. For more information on the changes in Nokia&#146;s leadership during 2014,
refer to &#147;&#151;Board of Directors and management&#151;Changes in Nokia Group Leadership&#148; below. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="12" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#150;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Nokia announced its new strategy building on Nokia&#146;s three businesses: Nokia Networks, HERE and Nokia Technologies. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD WIDTH="12" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">&#150;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Nokia announced plans for a EUR&nbsp;5 billion capital structure optimization program focused on recommencing dividend payments, distributing excess capital to shareholders, and reducing interest-bearing debt. Later in
the second quarter 2014, as part of this program and its debt reduction plan, Nokia redeemed approximately EUR&nbsp;950&nbsp;million of Nokia Networks debt, which included EUR&nbsp;800&nbsp;million of senior notes issued by Nokia Solutions and
Networks Finance B.V., the finance company of its Nokia Networks business. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">In May 2014, Nokia&#146;s credit rating was upgraded by credit rating agencies Moody&#146;s and Standard&nbsp;&amp; Poor&#146;s, supporting Nokia&#146;s long-term target to re-establish its investment grade credit
rating. Standard&nbsp;&amp; Poor&#146;s upgraded Nokia&#146;s rating to BB from B+, with a positive outlook, and Moody&#146;s upgraded Nokia&#146;s rating to Ba2 from B1 and added a positive outlook in November 2014. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">In May 2014, Nokia launched a USD 100&nbsp;million Connected Car Fund managed by Nokia Growth Partners, its venture capital arm. The fund will identify and invest in companies whose innovations are deemed important for
a world of connected and intelligent vehicles. The fund, working closely with Nokia&#146;s HERE business, will seek to make investments that also support the growth of the ecosystem around HERE&#146;s mapping and location products and services.
</TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Nokia&#146;s Annual General Meeting, which was held on June&nbsp;17, 2014, resolved to distribute an ordinary dividend of EUR&nbsp;0.11&nbsp;per share for year 2013 and a special dividend of EUR&nbsp;0.26&nbsp;per
share. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">In June 2014, Nokia ranked sixth in Interbrand&#146;s annual Best Global Green Brands report for 2014, measuring the environmental sustainability performance of leading global brands. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<P STYLE="margin-top:24pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Innovations for more efficient networks </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our
next generation technology innovations are designed to help operators carry more data in their networks and dramatically reduce their costs as, after cash they improve network efficiency and make better use of existing capacity. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:16pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#333333">&#147;Nokia announced its new strategy building on Nokia&#146;s three businesses: Nokia Networks, HERE and Nokia Technologies.&#148;
