 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance statement continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:14pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d">The current members of the Board were elected at the Annual General Meeting held on June&nbsp;17,
2014, based on the proposal of the Board&#146;s Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On the same date, the Chairman of the Board and Vice Chairman
of the Board of Directors, as well as the chairmen and members of the committees of the Board, were elected from among the Board members and among the independent directors of the Board, respectively. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The members of the Board are elected annually for a term ending at the close of the next Annual General Meeting. The election is made by a simple majority of the
shareholders&#146; votes cast at the Annual General Meeting. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_94"></A>Members of the Board of Directors </B></P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance statement continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Members of the Board of Directors continued </B></P>

<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Retired CEO of Verizon Wireless, Author and Consultant. Board member since
June&nbsp;17, 2014. Member of the Personnel Committee.</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Doctorate, Humane Letters (Honorary) Canisius College, Master of Business Administration (MBA) Farleigh

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>The following individuals served on Nokia Board until the close of the Annual General Meeting
held on June&nbsp;17, 2014: </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="19%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="5%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="76%"></TD></TR>


<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000"><B>Henning Kagermann,&nbsp;b. 1947</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000">Board member 2007-2014. Served as the chairman of the Personnel Committee and as a member of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee until June&nbsp;17, 2014.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>Helge&nbsp;Lund,&nbsp;b. 1962</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Board member 2011-2014. Served as a member of the Personnel Committee and as a member of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee until June 17,
2014.</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Proposal of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee for Composition of the Board of Directors in 2015 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On January&nbsp;29, 2015, the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee announced its proposal to the Annual General Meeting convening on May&nbsp;5, 2015 regarding
the composition of the Board for the one-year term starting from the Annual General Meeting in 2015 until the close of the Annual General Meeting in 2016. The Committee proposed that the number of Board members be eight and that the following
current members of the Board be re-elected as members of the Nokia Board for a term until the close of the Annual General Meeting in 2016: Vivek Badrinath, Bruce Brown, Elizabeth Doherty, Jouko Karvinen, Elizabeth Nelson, Risto Siilasmaa and Kari
Stadigh. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In addition, the Committee proposed that Simon Jiang be elected as a new member of the Board for the same term until the close of the Annual General
Meeting in 2016. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Election of the Chairman of the Board of Directors and Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors and the chairmen and members of the Board&#146;s
Committees </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Chairman of the Board and the Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors are elected from among the members of the Board by the new Board and
confirmed by the independent directors of the Board based on the recommendation of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee. The independent directors of the new Board also confirm the election of the members and chairmen for the
Board&#146;s committees from among the Board&#146;s independent directors upon the recommendation of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee and based on each committee&#146;s member qualification standards. These elections will take place
at the Board&#146;s assembly meeting following the Annual General Meeting in 2015. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On January&nbsp;29, 2015, the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee
announced that it will propose, in the assembly meeting of the new Board of Directors after the Annual General Meeting on May&nbsp;5, 2015, that Risto Siilasmaa be elected as Chairman of the Board and Jouko Karvinen as Vice Chairman of the Board of
Directors, subject to their election to the Board. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:14pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d">According to Nokia&#146;s Articles of Association, the Nokia Group Leadership Team (until
May&nbsp;1, 2014 the Nokia Leadership Team) is responsible for the operative management of Nokia. The Chairman and members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team are appointed by the Board. Rajeev Suri is the President and CEO of Nokia Corporation and
he also chairs the Nokia Group Leadership Team. </FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_95"></A>Members of Nokia Group Leadership Team </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Timo Ihamuotila served as interim President from September&nbsp;3, 2013 through to April&nbsp;30, 2014, while also continuing to serve as Chief Financial Officer.
During this interim time Mr.&nbsp;Ihamuotila also chaired the Nokia Leadership Team. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">During 2014, the following appointments were made to the Nokia Group
Leadership Team: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_97"></A>Board of Directors </B></P>

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="13" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(1)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The changes in the aggregate Board compensation year on year are attributable to changes in the number of Board members and their committee memberships. The compensation paid for services rendered remained the same over
the relevant periods. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In accordance with Nokia&#146;s policy, directors&#146; remuneration consists only of an annual fee and no additional fees are
paid for meeting attendance. Approximately 40% of the directors&#146; remuneration is paid in the form of Nokia shares that are purchased from the market or alternatively by using treasury shares held by the company. The rest of the remuneration is
paid in cash, most of which is typically used to cover taxes arising from the remuneration. In addition, directors shall retain all Nokia shares received as director compensation until the end of their Board membership (except for those shares
needed to offset any costs relating to the acquisition of the shares, including taxes). Non-executive directors do not participate in any of Nokia&#146;s equity programs and do not receive performance shares, restricted shares or any other equity
based or otherwise variable compensation for their duties as Board members. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The compensation of the Board is resolved annually by our shareholders at the Annual
General Meeting. It is resolved by a majority vote of the shareholders represented at the Annual General Meeting, upon the proposal of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee of the Board. The compensation is determined as of the date of
the Annual General Meeting until the close of the next Annual General Meeting. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">When preparing the proposal for Board compensation for the Annual General Meeting,
the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee reviews and compares total compensation levels and their criteria to other global peer group companies with net sales and a complexity of business comparable to that of Nokia&#146;s. The Corporate
Governance and Nomination Committee&#146;s aim is to ensure that Nokia has an efficient Board of international professionals representing a diverse mix of skills and experience. Competitive Board remuneration contributes to the achievement of this
target. </P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Compensation of the Board of Directors in 2014 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014, the aggregate amount of compensation paid to the members of the Board for their services as members of the Board and its committees equaled EUR
1&nbsp;580&nbsp;000. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The following table outlines the total annual compensation paid to the members of the Board for their services in 2014, as resolved by
shareholders at the Annual General Meeting on June&nbsp;17, 2014. For more details on Nokia shares held by the members of the Board, refer to &#147;&#151; Share ownership of the Board of Directors and the Nokia Group Leadership Team&#151;Share
ownership of the Board of Directors&#148; below. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">

 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Proposal by the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee for compensation to the Board of Directors in 2015
</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">On January&nbsp;29, 2015, the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee of the Board announced its proposal to the Annual General Meeting convening on
May&nbsp;5, 2015 regarding the remuneration to the Board in 2015. The Committee will propose that the annual fee payable to the Board members elected at the same meeting for a term until the close of the Annual General Meeting in 2016 remains at the
same level as it has been for the past seven years and be as follows: EUR&nbsp;440&nbsp;000 for the Chairman, EUR 150&nbsp;000 for the Vice Chairman and EUR&nbsp;130&nbsp;000 for each member; for the Chairman of the Audit Committee and the Chairman
of the Personnel Committee an additional annual fee of EUR 25&nbsp;000, and for each member of the Audit Committee an additional annual fee of EUR 10&nbsp;000. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The
guiding principle of the Corporate Governance and Nomination Committee&#146;s remuneration proposal is to align the interests of the directors with those of the shareholders by remunerating directors primarily with Nokia shares that according to the
current policy shall be retained for the duration of the Board membership. Therefore, the Committee will propose that, as in the past, approximately 40% of the Board remuneration be paid in Nokia shares purchased from the market or alternatively by
using treasury shares held by the company. The shares shall be retained until the end of a director&#146;s Board membership in line with the current Nokia policy (except for those shares needed to offset any costs relating to the acquisition of the
shares, including taxes). The rest of the remuneration would be payable in cash, most of which is typically used to cover taxes arising from the remuneration. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The
Committee&#146;s aim is to ensure that Nokia has an efficient Board of international professionals representing a diverse mix of skills and experience. A competitive Board remuneration contributes to the achievement of this target. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In determining the proposed remuneration, it is the Committee&#146;s policy to review and compare the total remuneration levels and their criteria paid in other global
companies with net sales and complexity of business comparable to that of Nokia&#146;s. It is the company&#146;s policy that the remuneration consists of an annual fee only, and that no fees for meeting attendance are paid. It is also the
company&#146;s policy that a significant portion of director compensation will be paid in the form of company shares purchased from the market or by using shares held by the company and that each Board member shall retain, in accordance with the
current policy, all Nokia shares received as director compensation until the end of his or her Board membership (except for those shares needed to offset any costs relating to the acquisition of the shares, including taxes). In addition, it is
Nokia&#146;s policy that non-executive members of the Board do not participate in any of its equity programs and do not receive stock options, performance shares, restricted shares or any other equity-based or otherwise variable compensation for
their duties as Board members. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_98"></A>Executive compensation </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Introduction </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The year 2014 was one of fundamental change for Nokia.
Following the Sale of the D&amp;S Business, Nokia emerged with three businesses &#150; Nokia Networks, HERE and Nokia Technologies. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As a result of these changes
and the new Nokia strategy we have introduced new corporate values and reviewed and refreshed our executive pay practices and policies for the Nokia Group Leadership Team.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Key updates made to our executive compensation practices are as follows: </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Drive performance </B></P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">We have updated the mix of various compensation elements to reflect market practice for companies of similar size and complexity. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Performance shares are now used as the primary vehicle for long-term incentives. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Attract and retain the right talent
</B></P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">We have updated our peer group for assessment of the competitiveness of our compensation packages and structure given our renewed business and strategy. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Align with shareholder interests </B></P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Our share ownership and clawback policies have been strengthened as part of the review of our compensation approach in order to ensure appropriate alignment with shareholders and accountability for sustainable long-term
company success. </TD></TR></TABLE></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Compensation continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>The Nokia Group Leadership Team </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">This section provides details regarding our compensation strategy, philosophy, policies, programs and practices, including specific information related to the Nokia
Group Leadership Team. The following members were our named executive officers at December&nbsp;31, 2014: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Compensation philosophy, design and strategy </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our compensation programs are designed to attract, retain and motivate talent with the right mix of skills and capabilities and foster a pay for performance
environment. Reward is aligned with the company&#146;s strategy by adopting an appropriate mix of fixed and variable compensation to engage and motivate employees in the performance of the business and ensure alignment with shareholders. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">A single compensation framework is used across the entire Nokia Group with a varying mix of fixed and variable pay for each level. Higher levels of performance-based
pay and equity compensation are used to reward executives for delivering long-term sustainable growth and creating shareholder value. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia aims to provide a
globally competitive compensation offering compared to companies of similar size and complexity. In its determination of compensation for the President and CEO and the Nokia Group Leadership Team, the Personnel Committee of the Board considers the
compensation levels in other companies in high technology, telecommunications and internet services industries, as well as companies from other industries that are headquartered in Europe and the United States. The peer group is determined by the
Personnel Committee and was reviewed during 2014 to ensure that the new focus of the Nokia Group is appropriately reflected in the peer group following the Sale of the D&amp;S Business and integration of the Nokia Networks organization. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In designing Nokia&#146;s variable compensation programs key consideration is given to: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">incorporating specific performance measures that align directly with the execution of our strategy; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">delivering an appropriate amount of performance-related variable compensation for the achievement of strategic goals and financial targets in both the short and the long term; </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">appropriately balancing rewards between company and individual performance; and </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">fostering an ownership culture that promotes sustainability and long-term value creation that aligns the interests of participants with those of the shareholders.
</TD></TR></TABLE></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
</DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<TD WIDTH="51%"></TD>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Compensation structure and goal setting </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In line with our overall compensation philosophy our executives are rewarded using a mix of fixed and variable pay. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The elements of the compensation structure for the Nokia Group Leadership Team are further detailed below: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">


<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="2%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="44%"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" WIDTH="1%"></TD>
<TD WIDTH="41%"></TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>Element</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>Principles</B></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-TOP:1px solid #000000; BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"><B>Purpose</B></TD></TR>


<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Base salary</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Fixed cash component targeted at our peer group median; base salary can vary from the market due to individual performance, experience, time in position, and internal equity considerations.
Base salaries are reviewed annually taking into account market conditions, affordability and individual performance.</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To compensate for the relevant knowledge, skills and experience the individual brings to the role and the responsibility of their position. Provides a degree of financial certainty and
stability that helps us retain talent.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Short-term incentive</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">An annual cash award designed to reward a mix of corporate, business unit, and individual performance compared to pre-established performance goals. The on target short-term incentive award,
when taken together with base salary, is designed to provide a median annual total cash compensation comparable to that of our peer group.</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Reward for the achievement of key business metrics by meeting financial and strategic targets during the fiscal year.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Long-term incentive</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The equity-based portion of compensation that is tied to Nokia&#146;s long-term success and delivered through
performance shares.</P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Long-term incentive awards are intended to provide competitive incentive
compensation compared with our peer group when combined with base salary and target short-term incentive.</P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The ultimate value of an award depends on our share price and business performance against predetermined performance measures.</P>
<P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Restricted shares are also used selectively to recruit and retain key talent on a very limited
basis. The number of shares vesting is predetermined but the ultimate value will rise or fall in line with movements in the share price.</P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:1pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">There are also certain legacy equity compensation programs in force as described in &#147;&#151;Legacy equity compensation programs&#148; below.</P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To reward for delivery of sustainable long-term performance, align the executives&#146; interests with those of shareholders and aid retention.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Benefits&nbsp;&amp; perquisites</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">The Nokia Group Leadership Team members are provided the same benefits that are made available to employees more broadly in the relevant country, with additional security provisions, as
appropriate. The Nokia Group Leadership Team members may also be provided with certain other benefits from time to time, which are not material in terms of monetary value.</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Benefits and perquisites are offered as part of the core compensation package to enable us to attract, retain and protect employees and executives.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Relocation&nbsp;&amp; mobility</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To facilitate international mobility by providing relevant benefits to assist executives in relocation. Mobility policies support the relocation of an executive and their dependents or the
reasonable costs of commuting. Benefits are market specific and are not compensation for performing the role but provided to defray costs or additional burdens of a relocation or residence outside the home country.</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To assist with mobility across the Nokia Group to ensure the appropriate talent is available to execute our strategy in the right locations.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Retirement plans</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To provide retirement funding in line with local market and legal requirements, typically through defined contribution or locally mandated pension plans. No supplemental pension arrangements
are provided.</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To give a market competitive level of provision for post-retirement income.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Change of control arrangements</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Change of control arrangements are offered on a very limited basis only, and based on a double trigger
structure, which means that both a specified change of control event and termination of the individual&#146;s employment must take place for any change of control based severance payment to materialize. More information provided under section</P>
<P STYLE="font-size:1pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:1pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">&#147;&#151;Members Termination provisions for the Nokia Group Leadership Team
members&#148;.</P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">To ensure the continuity of management in connection with possible change of control event.</TD></TR>
</TABLE>
 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Compensation mix </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The target mix of

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Long-term incentives </B></FONT></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Our long-term incentives are designed to ensure alignment with the interests of shareholders and the delivery of sustainable success at a corporate level. Long-term
incentive awards were predominantly made in performance shares, the details of which are discussed in more detail in the &#147;&#151;Equity compensation&#148; section below. In addition to the target level of long-term incentive award, additional
one time awards were made to Mr.&nbsp;Suri and Mr.&nbsp;Elhage. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Pension arrangements for the members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team
</B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The President and CEO and other members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team participate in the local retirement plans applicable to employees in the
country of residence. Executives based in Finland, Mr.&nbsp;Suri, Mr. Ihamuotila and Mr. Elhage, participate in the statutory Finnish pension system, as regulated by the Finnish Employees&#146; Pension Act (395/2006, as amended) (the &#147;Finnish
TyEL&#148;), which provides for a retirement benefit based on years of service and earnings according to prescribed rules. No supplemental pension arrangements are provided. Under the Finnish TyEL pension system, base pay, incentives and other
taxable fringe benefits are included in the definition of earnings, while gains realized from equity are not. Retirement benefits are available from age 63 to 68, according to an increasing scale. Mr&nbsp;Haidamus participates in Nokia&#146;s US
Retirement Savings and Investment Plan. Under this 401(k) plan, participants elect to make voluntary pre-tax contributions that are 100% matched by Nokia up to 8% of eligible earnings. 25% of the employer&#146;s match vests for the participants
annually during first four years of their employment. Mr. Fernback participates in the HERE Pension Plan that is 100% company funded. Contributions are based on pensionable earnings, the pension table and retirement age. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><FONT COLOR="#4d4d4d"><B>Other arrangements </B></FONT></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In line with Nokia&#146;s
high ethical standards, Nokia has adopted a new more stringent clawback policy. According to this policy, variable pay can be clawed back from executives, to whom the policy applies, in the event of a specified misconduct or a materially adverse
misstatement. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_99"></A>Compensation governance practices </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>The Board of Directors </B></P> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Approves and the independent members of the Board confirm the compensation of the President and CEO upon recommendation of the Personnel Committee; </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">approves upon recommendation from the Personnel Committee any long-term incentive compensation, and all equity plans, programs or similar arrangements of significance that the company establishes for its employees; and
</TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">decides on the issuance of shares to fulfill the company&#146;s obligations under equity plans in respect of vested awards to be settled. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>The Personnel Committee </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As part of its responsibilities the Personnel
Committee assists the Board in discharging its responsibilities relating to all
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">
compensation, including equity compensation, of the Company&#146;s executives and the terms of employment of the same, making recommendations to the Board: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">recommends to the Board the corporate goals and objectives relevant to the compensation of the President and CEO, and evaluates the performance of the President and CEO against previously established goals and
objectives as well as proposes to the Board the compensation level of the President and CEO; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">reviews and approves changes to the peer group for assessment of the competitiveness of our compensation from time to time; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">approves and oversees recommendations from the President and CEO for compensation for other members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team and any other executive-level direct reports to the CEO; </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">reviews and approves goals and objectives relevant to the compensation for other members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team and any other executive-level direct reports to the CEO, and reviews the results of the
evaluation of their performance in relation to the approved goals and objectives; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">reviews and periodically makes recommendations to the Board regarding the operation and amendment of any long-term incentive arrangements and all equity plans; and </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">reviews the content of and ensuring compliance with the share ownership policy. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Independent consultant </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Personnel Committee retains the use of an independent external consultant to assist in the review and determination of executive compensation. The consultant works
directly with the Personnel Committee and meets annually with the Committee, without management present to provide advice on: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">market data and appropriateness of compensation information compiled by management; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">the appropriateness and competitiveness of our compensation program relative to market levels and practice; and </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">executive compensation trends and developments. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Committee has reviewed and established that the consultant that
works for the Personnel Committee is independent of Nokia and does not have any other business relationships with Nokia. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>President and CEO </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The President and CEO plays an active role in compensation governance and performance management processes for his direct reports and the wider employee population in
Nokia. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The President and CEO is not a member of the Personnel Committee and does not vote at Personnel Committee meetings nor does he participate in any
conversations regarding his own compensation. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Compensation continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_100"></A>Service contract of President and CEO Rajeev Suri, effective as of May&nbsp;1, 2014 </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Pursuant to his service contract Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s annual base salary, which is subject to annual review by the Board and confirmation by the independent members of
the Board, is EUR 1&nbsp;000&nbsp;000 and his incentive target under the Nokia short-term cash incentive plan is 125% of annual base salary. Mr.&nbsp;Suri is entitled to the customary benefits in line with our policies applicable to the senior
executives, however, some of the benefits are being provided to him on a tax assisted basis. Mr.&nbsp;Suri is also eligible to participate in Nokia Group&#146;s long-term equity based compensation programs in accordance with Nokia policies and
guidelines and as determined by the Board. Pursuant to his service contract Mr. Suri maintained his participation in the Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan, which is further detailed in the &#147;&#151;Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan&#148;
section below. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s service contract may be terminated as follows: </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">termination by Nokia for reasons other than cause. In the event of a termination by Nokia for reasons other than cause, Mr.&nbsp;Suri is entitled to a severance payment equaling up to 18 months of compensation
(including annual base salary, benefits, and target incentive), and his unvested equity awards will be forfeited; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">termination by Nokia for cause. In the event of a termination by Nokia for cause, Mr.&nbsp;Suri is entitled to no additional compensation and all his unvested equity awards will be forfeited; </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">termination by Mr.&nbsp;Suri for any reason. Mr.&nbsp;Suri may terminate his service contract at any time with six months&#146; prior notice. Nokia may choose to pay a lump sum payment in lieu of his service during the
notice period or ask Mr.&nbsp;Suri to continue his service through all or part of this notice period. In either event, Mr.&nbsp;Suri is entitled to six months of compensation (including annual base salary, benefits, and target incentive), and his
unvested equity awards will be forfeited; </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">termination by Mr.&nbsp;Suri for Nokia&#146;s material breach of the service contract. In the event that Mr.&nbsp;Suri terminates his service contract based on a final arbitration award demonstrating Nokia&#146;s
material breach of the service contract, he is entitled to a severance payment equaling to up to 18 months of compensation (including annual base salary, benefits, and target incentive), and all his unvested equity awards will be forfeited; or
</TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left"><FONT STYLE="font-family:Times New Roman"><FONT STYLE="FONT-FAMILY:'WINGDINGS 2'">&#161;</FONT></FONT></TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">termination based on specified events. Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s service contract includes special severance provisions on a termination following a change of control event. Such change of control provisions are based on a
double trigger structure, which means that both a change of control event and the termination of the individual&#146;s employment within a defined period of time must take place in order for any change of control based severance payment to become
payable. More specifically, if a change of control event has occured, as defined in the service contract, and Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s service with Nokia is terminated either by Nokia or its successor without cause, or by Mr.&nbsp;Suri for &#147;good
reason&#148;, in either case within 18 months from such change of control event, Mr.&nbsp;Suri will be entitled to a severance payment equaling up to 18 months of compensation (including annual base salary, benefits, and target incentive) and cash
payment (or payments) for the pro-rated value of his
</TD></TR></TABLE></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">


<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="11">&nbsp;</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top"> <P STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
outstanding unvested equity awards, including equity awards under the Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan, restricted shares, performance shares and stock options (if any), payable pursuant to
the terms of the service contract. &#147;Good reasons&#148; referred to above include a material reduction of Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s compensation and a material reduction of his duties and responsibilities, as defined in the service contract and as
determined by the Board. </P></TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In addition, the service contract defines a specific, limited termination event that applies until June&nbsp;30, 2016.
Upon this event, if Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s service with Nokia is terminated as a result of the circumstances specified in the service contract, he is entitled to, in addition to normal severance payment payable upon his termination by Nokia for
reasons other than cause, to a pro rated value of unvested equity awards under the Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan, provided that the termination of his service takes place within six months from the defined termination event (and at or prior
to June&nbsp;30, 2016). Subject to this limited time treatment of unvested equity awards under the Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan, all of Mr.&nbsp;Suri&#146;s other unvested equity will be forfeited. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Mr. Suri is subject to a 12-month non-competition obligation that applies after the termination of the service contract or the date when he is released from his
obligations and responsibilities, whichever occurs earlier. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_101"></A>Termination provisions for the Nokia Group Leadership Team members
</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Maintaining a stable and effective leadership team is considered essential for protecting and enhancing the best interests of Nokia and its shareholders. In
order to encourage the continued attention, dedication and continuity of the members of the Nokia Group Leadership Team to their assigned duties without the distraction that may arise from the possibility of termination of employment as a result of
a specified change of control event in Nokia, certain provisions have been made available to them. The provisions&nbsp;for the President and CEO are described in the &#147;Service contract of President and CEO Rajeev Suri, effective as of May 1,
2014&#148; section. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In all cases, if an executive is dismissed for cause, then no compensation will be payable and no outstanding equity will vest. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In the event of termination for any other reason than cause, where the company pays compensation in lieu of notice period&#146;s salary, benefits and target short-term
incentive amounts are taken into account. In addition, special provisions exist for the treatment of equity awards granted prior to the Sale of the D&amp;S Business for Mr. Ihamuotila in the event that Nokia terminates his service contract for
reasons other than cause, death or retirement. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Nokia Group Leadership Team members have change of control agreements with Nokia, which serve as an addendum to
their service contracts. These change of control agreements are based on a double trigger structure, which means that both the change of control event and the termination of the individual&#146;s employment must take place for any change of control
based severance payment to materialize. More specifically, if a change of control event, as defined in the agreement, has occurred in the company, and the individual&#146;s employment with the company is terminated either by Nokia or its successor
without cause, or by the individual for &#147;good reason&#148; (for example, material reduction of duties and responsibilities), in either case within 18 months from such change of control event, the individual will be entitled to his or her notice
period compensation (including base salary, benefits, and target incentive) and cash payment (or payments) for the pro-rated value of the individual&#146;s outstanding unvested equity, including restricted shares, performance shares, stock options
and equity awards under Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan, payable pursuant to the terms of the agreement. The Board of Directors has the full discretion to terminate or amend the change of control agreements at any time.
</P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>

 <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Compensation continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_102"></A>Summary compensation table </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Summary Compensation Table below reflects the cash compensation and benefits earned in 2014 and the value of long-term incentive awards made to the Nokia Group
Leadership Team members serving as of the end of 2014. In addition, the Summary Compensation Table sets forth the compensation of each other individual who served as Chief Executive Officer or Chief Financial Officer at any point during the year, as
well as up to two other Nokia Group Leadership Team members who would have been among the three most highly compensated had they still been serving Nokia Group Leadership Team members at year-end. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">A significant portion of equity grants presented in the below Summary Compensation Table to the Nokia Group Leadership Team members are tied to the performance of the
company and aligned with the value delivered to shareholders. Therefore, the amounts shown are not representative of the amounts that will actually be earned and paid out to each Nokia Group Leadership Team member (but rather the accounting grant
date fair value of each applicable grant, which is required to be reported in the Summary Compensation Table). In fact, for each of the years reported, the compensation &#147;realized&#148; by each Nokia Group Leadership Team member is lower than
the amount required to be reported in the Summary Compensation Table. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(3)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Amounts shown represent the grant date fair value of equity grants awarded for the respective fiscal year. The fair value of stock options equals the estimated fair value on the grant date, calculated using the
Black-Scholes model. The fair value of performance shares and restricted shares equals the estimated fair value on grant date. The estimated fair value is based on the grant date market price of a Nokia share less the present value of dividends
expected to be paid during the vesting period. The value of the performance shares is presented on the basis of granted number of shares, which is two times the number of shares at threshold. The value of the stock awards with performance shares
valued at maximum (four times the number of shares at threshold), for each of the named executive officers, is as follows: Mr.&nbsp;Suri EUR 7&nbsp;519&nbsp;872; Mr.&nbsp;Ihamuotila EUR 1&nbsp;908&nbsp;888; Mr.&nbsp;Elhage EUR 2&nbsp;776&nbsp;576;
Mr.&nbsp;Fernback EUR 986&nbsp;903; and Mr.&nbsp;Haidamus EUR 1&nbsp;432&nbsp;440. A significant portion of these equity grants to the Nokia Group Leadership Team is tied to Nokia&#146;s performance and aligned with the value delivered to
shareholders. Therefore, the amounts shown are not representative of the amounts that will actually be earned and paid out to each named executive officer (but rather the accounting grant date fair value of each applicable grant, which is required
to be reported in the Summary Compensation Table). </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

mandated TyEL pension arrangements. Executives in the United States participate in the plan described in footnote 10 to this table. Mr Fernback is a participant in the Nokia Germany Defined Benefit pension plan where the pension is determined by

by Nokia up to 8% of eligible earnings. 25% of the employer&#146;s match vests for the participants during each of the first four years of their employment. Participants earning in excess of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) eligible earning limits
may participate in the Nokia Restoration and Deferral Plan, which allows employees to defer up to 50% of their salary and 100% of their short-term cash incentive. Contributions to the Restoration and Deferral Plan are matched 100% up to 8% of
eligible earnings, less contributions made to the 401(k) plan. Nokia&#146;s contributions to the plan are included under &#147;All Other Compensation Column&#148; and noted hereafter. </TD></TR></TABLE>

<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The fair value of performance shares and restricted shares equals the estimated fair value on grant date. The estimated fair value is based on the grant date market price of the Nokia share less the present value of
dividends expected to be paid during the vesting. The value of performance shares is presented on the basis of a number of shares, which is two times the number at threshold. </TD></TR></TABLE>
<P STYLE="margin-top:10pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_103"></A>Equity compensation </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Equity compensation
program </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Nokia operates a number of equity programs, with the common purpose of aligning the participants&#146; interests with those of shareholders. All
programs require continued employment with Nokia for the awards to vest. In line with our pay for performance philosophy, the principle equity vehicle is the Performance Share Plan, which includes defined performance conditions linked to
Nokia&#146;s long-term success. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The active equity plans in 2014 can be summarized as follows: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Annual long-term incentive awards, to reward for delivery of sustainable long-term performance, align with the interests shareholders and aid retention of key employees</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Exceptional recruitment and retention</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Encourage share ownership within the Nokia employee population, increasing engagement and sense of ownership in the company.</TD></TR>
<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"> <P STYLE="margin-left:0.50em; text-indent:-0.50em; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Vesting schedule</P></TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Two-year performance period and further one-year holding requirement</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Vest on the third anniversary of grant</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" STYLE="BORDER-BOTTOM:1px solid #000000">Matching shares vest at the end of the 12-month savings period</TD></TR>
</TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In addition, Nokia also has two stock option plans and the Nokia Networks Equity Incentive Plan that are no longer used to grant new
awards but under which there are outstanding awards from earlier years. These are described in the section on legacy equity compensation programs. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Performance
share grants to the President and CEO are approved by the Board and confirmed by the independent directors of the Board upon recommendation by the Personnel Committee. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Performance share grants to the other Nokia Group Leadership Team members and other direct reports of the President and CEO are approved by the Personnel Committee.
</P>

<TR STYLE="font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt">
<TD VALIGN="bottom">NOKIA ANNUAL REPORT ON FORM 20-F 2014</TD>
<TD VALIGN="bottom"></TD>
<TD VALIGN="top" ALIGN="right">112</TD></TR></TABLE>


<p Style='page-break-before:always'>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Corporate governance </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Equity awards to other eligible employees are approved by the President and CEO on a quarterly basis, based on an
authorization given by the Board. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">At December&nbsp;31, 2014, the total dilutive effect of all Nokia&#146;s stock options, performance shares and restricted shares
outstanding, assuming full dilution, was approximately 1.37% in the aggregate. The potential maximum effect of the 2015 Equity Plans would be approximately another 0.97%. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Performance shares </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The performance shares represent a commitment by Nokia
to deliver Nokia shares to employees at a future point in time, subject to Nokia&#146;s fulfillment of pre-defined performance criteria. The shares have a two-year performance period followed by a further one-year restriction period after which the
awards vest. The table below illustrates the performance criteria of the Performance Share Plans as of 2012 through to 2014. </P> <P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(1)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Non-IFRS measures exclude all material special items for all periods. In addition, non-IFRS results exclude intangible asset amortization and other purchase price accounting-related items arising from business
acquisitions. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(2)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">The performance condition was amended at the time of the Sale of the D&amp;S Business to reflect the new profile of the business and different annual revenue levels of the new business. The amendment introduces a metric
set on the basis of the Average Net Sales Index over the two-year performance period in replacement of the metric set on the basis of the Average Annual Net Sales Revenue. The &#145;Net Sales Index&#146; relates to the final non-IFRS annual net
sales achieved through the business operations of Nokia Group (excluding Nokia Networks) in relation to 2013 and for Nokia Networks, HERE and Nokia Technologies in relation to 2014, expressed as a percentage of the annual target set for each year. A
separate Annual Net Sales Index will be calculated for 2013 and 2014, and the average of the two will be calculated following the close of 2014 and used, in part, to determine the final payout under the Plan, which will occur after the one-year
restriction period in 2016. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(3)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">Specific performance criteria for HERE employees were introduced in 2014. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="font-size:2pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6pt" BORDER="0" CELLPADDING="0" CELLSPACING="0" WIDTH="100%">
<TR>
<TD WIDTH="2%" VALIGN="top" ALIGN="left">(4)</TD>
<TD ALIGN="left" VALIGN="top">In 2014, a minimum payout level was introduced to reinforce the retentive impact of the plan by giving some certainty to remaining employees during the transformation of Nokia following the Sale of the D&amp;S Business
and integration of the Nokia Networks business. </TD></TR></TABLE> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Until the shares have vested and been delivered to the participants, they carry no voting or dividend
rights. The performance share grants are generally forfeited if the employment relationship terminates with Nokia prior to vesting. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Performance Share Plan 2015
</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The primary equity incentive instrument for the executives and other selected employees in 2015 will again be performance shares. The approximate maximum
numbers of planned grants under the Nokia Performance Share Plan 2015 is 32&nbsp;220&nbsp;000 units. The minimum number of grant units is 8&nbsp;055&nbsp;000. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">As
in 2014, the number of shares to be settled after the restriction period will start at 25% of the grant amount and any payout beyond this will be determined with reference to the financial performance against the established performance criteria
during the two-year performance period. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">The Nokia Performance Share Plan 2015 has a two-year performance period (2015 through to 2016) and a subsequent one-year
restriction period. Therefore, the amount of shares based on the financial performance during 2015&#150;2016 will vest after 2017. The performance criteria and range for Nokia Group employees (excluding HERE employees) are as follows: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:12pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Compensation continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Performance criteria are set with the purpose of being challenging but achievable to ensure that executives are
motivated. The awards at the threshold are significantly reduced from grant level and achievement of maximum award would require performance significantly ahead of current market expectations. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Achievement of the maximum performance for all criteria would result in the vesting of a maximum of 32.2&nbsp;million Nokia shares. Achievements beyond the maximum
performance level will not cause any further shares to vest. Achievement of the threshold performance for all criteria will result in the vesting of approximately 8.1&nbsp;million shares which is the minimum payout under the plan. Minimum payout
under the plan, even if threshold performance is not achieved, is 4.05&nbsp;million shares due to the 25% minimum payout. Until Nokia shares are delivered, the participants will not have any shareholder rights, such as voting or dividend rights
associated with these performance shares. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Restricted shares </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">In 2014
restricted shares were used on a selective basis to ensure retention and recruitment of individuals deemed critical to Nokia&#146;s future success. The restricted shares vest on the third anniversary of the award subject to continued employment with
Nokia. Until the restricted shares vest they carry no voting or dividend rights. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Restricted shares under the Nokia Restricted Share Plan 2015 will be used in an
increasingly targeted way. Grants will be focused on retention and recruitment of key individuals in defined locations where supported by local practice, for example in Silicon Valley and other parts of the United States where Nokia maintains a
significant presence. The shares will vest in three equal tranches over three years, on the first, second and third anniversary of the award. Vesting is subject to continued employment with the company. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Until the shares are delivered, the participants will not have any shareholder rights, such as voting or dividend rights, associated with the restricted shares. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Employee Share Purchase Plan </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the Employee Share Purchase Plan,
eligible Nokia employees can elect to make monthly contributions from their salary to purchase Nokia shares. The contribution per employee cannot exceed EUR 1&nbsp;200&nbsp;per year. The share purchases are made at market value on predetermined
dates on a monthly basis during a 12-month savings period. Nokia will offer one matching share for every two purchased shares the employee still holds after the last monthly purchase has been made following the end of the 12-month savings period.
Participation in the plan is voluntary to employees. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:10pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B><A NAME="tx861743_104"></A>Legacy equity compensation programs </B></P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">No new awards have been made under the following equity programs in 2014 but awards made in earlier years remain in force.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Stock options </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Although the
granting of stock options ceased at the end of 2013, awards under the 2007 and 2011 option plans remain in force. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL">Under the plans, each stock option entitles the
holder to subscribe for one new Nokia share and the stock options are non-transferable and may be exercised for shares only. The difference between the two plans is in the vesting schedule as follows: </P>
<P STYLE="font-size:6pt;margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:8.5pt" ALIGN="center">
