<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Other information </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Global Services:</B> A segment within Nokia Networks. Global Services provides mobile operators with a broad range of
services, including professional services, network implementation and customer care services. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>GPS (Global Positioning System):</B> Satellite-based positioning
system that is used for reading geographical position and as a source of the accurate coordinated universal time. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>GSM (Global System for Mobile
Communications):</B> A digital system for mobile communications that is based on a widely-accepted standard and typically operates in the 900 MHz, 1800 MHz and 1900 MHz frequency bands. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>HERE:</B> A Nokia company focused on location intelligence. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>HSPA (High-Speed
Packet Access):</B> A wideband code division multiple access (WCDMA or 3G) feature that refers to both 3GPP high-speed downlink packet access and high-speed uplink packet access. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Internet of Things: </B>All things such as cars, the clothes we wear, household appliances and machines in factories connected to the Internet and able to
automatically learn and organize themselves.<B> </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>ICT: </B>Information and communications technology. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Implementation patents:</B> Implementation patents include technologies used to implement functionalities in products or services which are not covered by
commitments to standards setting organizations, so they typically offer product differentiation by giving competitive advantage, such as increased performance, smaller size or improved battery life and the patent owner has no obligation to license
them to others. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Industrial design: </B>Design process applied for products that will be manufactured at mass scale. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Internet Protocol:</B> A network layer protocol that offers a connectionless internet work service and forms part of the TCP/IP protocol. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>IP (Intellectual Property):</B> Intellectual property results from original creative thought, covering items such as patents, copyright material, trademarks, as well
as business models and plans. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>IP Multimedia Subsystem:</B> Architectural framework designed to deliver IP-based multimedia services on telco networks;
standardized by 3GPP. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>IPR (Intellectual Property Right):</B> Legal right protecting the economic exploitation of intellectual property, a generic term used to
describe products of human intellect, for example patents, that have an economic value. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>IPR licensing:</B> Generally refers to an agreement or an arrangement
where a company allows another company to use its intellectual property (such as patents, trademarks or copyrights) under certain terms. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>IPTV (Internet Protocol
Television):</B> Television services delivered over Internet protocol infrastructure through a telephone or cable network using a broadband access line. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Java:</B> An object-oriented programming language that is intended to be hardware and software independent.
</P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>LTE (Long-Term Evolution):</B> 3GPP radio technology evolution architecture and a standard for wireless communication
of high-speed data. Also referred to as 4G. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>LTE-A (LTE Advanced):</B> The evolution of LTE that allows operators to use more than one spectrum band in parallel
and defines a set of techniques focused on enhancing the mobile broadband user experience, as well as reducing the cost per bit. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Microcell:</B> A cell in a
mobile phone network served by a low power cellular base station covering a limited area, typically up to two kilometers wide. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Mobile broadband:</B> Refers to
high-speed wireless internet connections and services designed to be used from arbitrary locations. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Mobile Broadband:</B> A segment within Nokia Networks.
Mobile Broadband provides mobile operators with radio and core network software together with the hardware needed to deliver mobile voice and data services. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Multiradio:</B> Able to support several different radio access technologies. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>NFC (Near Field Communication):</B> A short-range wireless technology that enables people to connect one NFC-enabled device with another, or to read an NFC tag. By
bringing one NFC-enabled mobile device close to another NFC device, or to an NFC tag, people can easily share content, access information and services, or pay for goods. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>NFV (Network Functions Virtualization): </B>Principle of separating network functions from the hardware they run on by using virtual hardware abstraction.<B>
</B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>NGOA (Next Generation Optical Access):</B> Future telecommunications system based on fiber optic cables capable of achieving bandwidth data rates greater
than 100 Mbps. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Nokia Networks:</B> A Nokia business focused on mobile network infrastructure software, hardware and services. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Nokia Technologies:</B> A Nokia business focused on advanced technology development and licensing. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>NSN:</B> Short for Nokia Solutions and Networks, the former name of our Nokia Networks business. From 2007, NSN was known as Nokia Siemens Networks until Nokia
acquired Siemens&#146; 50% stake in the joint venture in 2013. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency-Division Multiplexing):</B> A technique for transmitting large amounts
of digital data over a radio wave. OFDM works by splitting the radio signal into multiple smaller sub-signals that are then transmitted simultaneously at different frequencies to the receiver. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Open source:</B> Refers to a program in which the source code is available to the general public for use and modification from its original design free of charge.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Operating system (OS):</B> Software that controls the basic operation of a computer or a mobile device, such as managing the processor and memory. The term is
also often used to refer more generally to the software within a device, including, for instance, the user interface. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Packet:</B> Part of a message transmitted
over a packet switched network. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">

<h5 align="left"><a href="#toc">Table of Contents</a></h5>


 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:12pt; font-family:ARIAL">Glossary of terms continued </P> <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:left; margin-right:1%; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Picocell: </B>A small cellular base station typically covering a small area typically up to 200 meters wide. Typically
used to extend coverage to indoor areas or to add network capacity in areas with very dense phone usage, such as train stations. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Platform:</B> Software platform
is a term used to refer to an operating system or programming environment, or a combination of the two. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>PND (Personal navigation Device):</B> A term used to
describe portable devices or devices embedded in a vehicle that are used primarily for navigation. Smartphones and increasingly feature phones may include navigation functionalities, but are not generally referred to as PNDs. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Programmable World: </B>Our vision of the future; a world where connectivity will expand massively, linking people as well as hundreds of billions of physical
objects &#150; from cars, home appliances and smartphones, to wearables, industrial equipment and health monitors. What distinguishes the Programmable World from the Internet of Things is the intelligence that is added to data to allow people to
interpret and use it, rather than just capture it.<B> </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>RAN (Radio Access Network): </B>A mobile telecommunications system consisting of radio base stations
and transmission equipment.<B> </B></P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>SEPs (Standard-Essential Patents): </B>Generally, patents needed to produce products which work on a standard, which
companies declare as essential and agree to license on fair, reasonable and non-discriminatory (FRAND) terms. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Single RAN:</B> Single RAN allows different radio
technologies to be provided at the same time from a single base station, using a multi-purpose platform. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Small cells:</B> low-powered radio access nodes (micro
cells or picocell) and are a vital element to handling very dense data traffic demands. 3G and LTE small cells use spectrum licensed by the operator; WiFi uses unlicensed spectrum which is therefore not under the operator&#146;s exclusive control.
</P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Software Defined Networking:</B> An approach to computer networking that decouples the network control and forwarding functions enabling the network control to
become programmable and the underlying hardware to be abstracted. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>TD-LTE Time Division Long Term Evolution, also known as TDD (Time Division Duplex):</B> An
alternative standard for LTE mobile broadband networks. Time Division means that a single connection is used alternately to carry data from the base station to the mobile device (&#145;downlink&#146;) and then from the mobile device to the base
station (&#145;uplink&#146;). </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>TD-SCDMA (Time Division Synchronous Code Division Multiple Access):</B> An alternative 3G standard. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Technology licensing:</B> Generally refers to an agreement or arrangement where a company provides under certain terms another company its technology and possibly
know-how, whether protected by intellectual property or not, for use in products or services offered by the other company. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Telco Cloud: </B>Applying cloud
computing, SDN and NFV principles in telecommunications environment, e.g. separating application software from underlying hardware with automated, programmable interfaces while still retaining telecommunications requirements such as high
availability and low latency.<B> </B></P></DIV><DIV STYLE="position:relative;float:right; width:48%;padding-right:9pt;padding-bottom:8pt;overflow:hidden;padding-top:3pt">
 <P STYLE="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Transmission: </B>The action of conveying signals from one point to one or more other points. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>VDSL (Very High Bit Rate Digital Subscriber Line):</B> A form of digital subscriber line similar to asymmetric digital subscriber line (ADSL) but providing higher
speeds at reduced lengths. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>VoIP (Voice over Internet Protocol):</B> Use of the Internet protocol to carry and route two-way voice communications. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>VoLTE (Voice over LTE):</B> Required to offer voice services on an all-IP LTE network and generally provided using IP Multimedia Subsystem. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>WCDMA (Wideband Code Division Multiple Access):</B> A third-generation mobile wireless technology that offers high data speeds to mobile and portable wireless
devices. </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>Wi-Fi:</B> A technology enabling an electronic device to transfer data wirelessly over a network, including high-speed Internet connections. </P>
<P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>WiMAX (Worldwide Interoperability for Microwave Access):</B> A technology of wireless networks that operates according to the 802.16 standard of the Institute of
Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). </P> <P STYLE="margin-top:6pt; margin-bottom:0pt; font-size:8.5pt; font-family:ARIAL"><B>WLAN (Wireless Local Area Network):</B> A local area network using wireless connections, such as radio, microwave
or infrared links, in place of physical cables. </P></DIV><div style="clear:both; height:0pt; font-size:0pt">&nbsp;</div>

 <p STYLE="margin-top:0pt;margin-bottom:0pt ; font-size:8pt">&nbsp;</P>
<TABLE CELLSPACING="0" CELLPADDING="0" WIDTH="100%" BORDER="0" STYLE="BORDER-COLLAPSE:COLLAPSE; font-family:ARIAL; font-size:6.5pt" ALIGN="center">
